title.zh: 卷二 顯宗孝明帝紀
title.en: Volume 2: Annals of Emperor Xianzong Xiaoming
title.raw: 卷二 顯宗孝明帝紀 Volume 2: Annals of Emperor Xianzong Xiaoming

sentences.zh: 顯宗孝明皇帝諱莊，光武第四子也。

translations.literal: The Emperor with the temple name Xianzong and the posthumous title Xiaoming, whose tabooed personal name was Zhuang, was the fourth son of Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ming—temple name Xianzong, posthumous epithet Xiaoming—was Liu Zhuang, the fourth son of Emperor Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 母陰皇后。

translations.literal: His mother was Empress Yin.
translations.idiomatic: His mother was Empress Yin.

sentences.zh: 帝生而豐下，十歲能通《春秋》，光武奇之。

translations.literal: The emperor was born with a full lower jaw; at ten sui he could master the Spring and Autumn Annals, and Guangwu marveled at him.
translations.idiomatic: He was born with a prominent chin—an auspicious sign—and at the age of ten had mastered the Spring and Autumn Annals; Guangwu regarded him with wonder.

sentences.zh: 建武十五年封東海公，十七年進爵為王，十九年立為皇太子。

translations.literal: In the fifteenth year of Jianwu he was enfeoffed as duke of Donghai; in the seventeenth year his rank was advanced to king; in the nineteenth year he was established as heir apparent.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 15 he received the title Duke of Donghai; in Jianwu 17 he was raised to king; in Jianwu 19 he was named crown prince.

sentences.zh: 師事博士桓榮，學通《尚書》。

translations.literal: He took the Erudite Huan Rong as his teacher in the manner of a disciple and studied until he had mastered the Documents.
translations.idiomatic: He studied under the academician Huan Rong as a formal pupil and gained a thorough command of the Book of Documents.

sentences.zh: 中元二年二月戊戌，即皇帝位，年三十。

translations.literal: On the day wuxu in the second month of the second year of Zhongyuan, he assumed the imperial throne; he was thirty sui.
translations.idiomatic: On wuxu in the second month of Zhongyuan 2 (57 CE) he ascended the throne at the age of thirty.

sentences.zh: 尊皇后曰皇太后。

translations.literal: The empress was honored as empress dowager.
translations.idiomatic: The empress was elevated to empress dowager.

sentences.zh: 三月丁卯，葬光武皇帝於原陵。

translations.literal: On the day dingmao in the third month, Emperor Guangwu was buried at Yuanling.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month, on dingmao, Emperor Guangwu was interred at the Yuanling mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 有司奏上尊廟曰世祖。

translations.literal: The responsible officials memorialized, and the supreme temple title was approved as Shizu.
translations.idiomatic: Officials presented a memorial, and his dynastic temple name was fixed as Shizu.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙辰，詔曰：『予未小子，奉承聖業，夙夜震畏，不敢荒寧。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fourth month, on the day bingchen, an edict stated: "I am still a minor, receiving the sacred enterprise; morning and night I tremble in awe and dare not be idle or at ease.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of summer, on bingchen, the court issued an edict: "I am young and untried, yet I inherit this sacred charge; I tremble day and night and dare not grow complacent.

sentences.zh: 先帝受命中興，德侔帝王，協和萬邦，假於上下，懷柔百神，惠於鰥、寡。

translations.literal: The late emperor received the Mandate and revived the house; his virtue matched that of the sage-kings; he brought the myriad regions into harmony; he received favor from those above and below; he cherished and soothed the hundred spirits; he showed kindness to widowers, widows, orphans, and the childless.
translations.idiomatic: The late emperor received Heaven's mandate and restored the dynasty; his virtue rivalled the ancient kings; he harmonized the realm, won the trust of Heaven and earth, honored the gods, and cared for the widowed, the orphaned, and the alone.

sentences.zh: 朕承大運，繼體守文，不知稼穡之艱難，懼有廢失。

translations.literal: I have received the great fortune and succeed to the body [of the throne], preserving the pattern of culture; I do not know the hardships of sowing and reaping, and I fear there may be neglect and loss.
translations.idiomatic: I have inherited this great fortune and now uphold the civil order; I have not known the toil of the fields, and I fear I may fail in my duty.

sentences.zh: 聖恩遺戒，顧重天下，以元元為首。

translations.literal: The sage's grace left admonitions, looking gravely upon the empire and taking the common people as the first concern.
translations.idiomatic: His parting counsel weighed the whole realm and put the common people first.

sentences.zh: 公卿百僚，將何以輔朕不逮？

translations.literal: You, the dukes and ministers and the hundred officials—by what means will you assist me where I fall short?"
translations.idiomatic: How, then, will you ministers and officials help me where I am lacking?"

sentences.zh: 其賜天下男子爵，人二級；

translations.literal: Let there be granted to the men of the empire noble ranks, two grades per person;
translations.idiomatic: Let every man in the realm receive two steps in noble rank;

sentences.zh: 三老、孝悌、力田人三級；

translations.literal: to the village elders, the exemplars of filial piety and fraternal duty, and the champions of diligent farming, three grades per person;
translations.idiomatic: award three steps to each village elder, each recognized paragon of filial piety and brotherly love, and each outstanding farmer;

sentences.zh: 爵過公乘，得移與子若同產、同產子；

translations.literal: where rank exceeds gongcheng, it may be transferred to a son or to a full brother or a full brother's son;
translations.idiomatic: anyone whose rank already exceeds gongcheng may pass the excess to a son, a full brother, or a brother's son;

sentences.zh: 及流人無名數欲自占者人一級；

translations.literal: and to wandering people without registered names who wish to register themselves, one grade per person;
translations.idiomatic: and grant one step to vagrants without household registration who come forward to settle their status;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃粟，人十斛。

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, the solitary, and the seriously ill, grain, ten hu per person.
translations.idiomatic: distribute ten hu of grain to each widower, widow, orphan, person living alone, and person gravely disabled.

sentences.zh: 其弛刑及郡國徒，在中元元年四月己卯赦前所犯而後捕繫者，悉免其刑。

translations.literal: As for those under mitigated punishment and convicts laboring in the commanderies and principalities, for offenses committed before the general amnesty on the day jimao in the fourth month of the first year of Zhongyuan but seized afterward, entirely remit their punishments.
translations.idiomatic: Commute or pardon all convicts and corvée laborers in the provinces whose crimes predated the jimao amnesty of Zhongyuan 1 but who were arrested only later.

sentences.zh: 又邊人遭亂為內郡人妻，在己卯赦前，一切遣還邊，恣其所樂。

translations.literal: Moreover, border people who, in the turmoil, became wives of men in inner commanderies—if before the jimao amnesty, in every case send them back to the border and let them follow their own wishes.
translations.idiomatic: Border women who during the disorders had married into inner provinces and whose unions predated that same jimao amnesty shall all be free to return to their home regions if they wish.

sentences.zh: 中二千石下至黃綬，貶秩贖論者，悉皆復秩還贖。

translations.literal: From those of two thousand piculs down to holders of the yellow ribbon, those who suffered demotion in rank or were sentenced to ransom, in every case restore rank and return ransom payments.
translations.idiomatic: Officials from the rank of two thousand piculs down to those bearing the yellow seal who lost rank or paid fines to commute sentences shall have their former rank restored and their payments refunded.

sentences.zh: 方今上無天子，下無方伯，若涉淵水而無舟楫。

translations.literal: "At present above there is no Son of Heaven, below there is no regional lord; it is like wading into a deep pool without boat or oar.
translations.idiomatic: "Today, with no sovereign above and no strong regional power below, the state is like a man fording deep water without raft or oar.

sentences.zh: 夫萬乘至重而壯者慮輕，實賴有德左右小子。

translations.literal: The position of the myriad chariots is supremely weighty, yet the vigorous take their duties lightly; truly I depend on the virtuous to attend me, this minor."
translations.idiomatic: The throne is a burden almost too heavy to bear, yet the able-bodied often treat it lightly; I must rely on men of virtue to steady this young ruler."

sentences.zh: 高密侯禹，元功之首；

translations.literal: Marquis of Gaomi Yu is the foremost of the contributors to the founding achievement;
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu, Marquis of Gaomi, stands first among those who won the founding;

sentences.zh: 東平王蒼，寬博有謀；

translations.literal: the king of Dongping, Cang, is broad-minded and possessed of strategy;
translations.idiomatic: Liu Cang, king of Dongping, is generous in spirit and wise in counsel;

sentences.zh: 並可以受六尺之托，臨大節而不撓。

translations.literal: both may receive the charge of a six-foot orphan and, when great integrity is tested, not bend.
translations.idiomatic: either is fit to be entrusted with a child heir and to hold firm when crisis tries the realm.

sentences.zh: 其以禹為太傅，蒼為驃騎將軍。

translations.literal: Therefore appoint Yu as grand tutor and Cang as general-in-chief of agile cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: Appoint Yu grand tutor and Cang general-in-chief of agile cavalry.

sentences.zh: 大尉憙告謚南郊，司徒訢奉安梓宮，司空魴將校復土。

translations.literal: Let the grand commandant Xi announce the posthumous title at the southern suburb; let the minister of education Xin install the inner coffin in the mausoleum; let the minister of works Fang lead the officers in the backfilling of the tomb.
translations.idiomatic: The grand commandant Xi shall proclaim the posthumous name at the southern suburb; the minister of education Xin shall oversee placement of the inner coffin; the minister of works Fang shall command the burial squad that replaces the earth.

sentences.zh: 其封憙為節鄉侯，訢為安鄉侯，魴為楊邑侯。』

translations.literal: Enfeoff Xi as marquis of Jiexiang, Xin as marquis of Anxiang, and Fang as marquis of Yangyi."
translations.idiomatic: Enfeoff Xi as Marquis of Jiexiang, Xin as Marquis of Anxiang, and Fang as Marquis of Yangyi."

sentences.zh: 秋九月，燒當羌寇隴西，敗郡兵於允街。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the ninth month, the Shao Dang Qiang raided Longxi and defeated the commandery troops at Yunjie.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, the ninth month, the Shao Dang Qiang struck Longxi and routed local forces at Yunjie.

sentences.zh: 赦隴西囚徒，減罪一等，勿收今年租調。

translations.literal: The convicts of Longxi were amnestied; their crimes were reduced one degree, and this year's land tax and labor surcharges were not to be collected.
translations.idiomatic: Prisoners in Longxi were pardoned one degree, and the year's rent and irregular levies were waived.

sentences.zh: 又所發天水三千人，亦復是歲更賦。

translations.literal: Moreover, the three thousand men levied from Tianshui likewise had that year's rotating labor tax remitted.
translations.idiomatic: The three thousand conscripts raised from Tianshui were likewise excused from the rotating labor levy for that year.

sentences.zh: 遣謁者張鴻討叛羌於允吾，鴻軍大敗，戰歿。

translations.literal: The yeoman Zhang Hong was sent to attack the rebel Qiang at Yunwu; Hong's army suffered a great defeat, and he died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: Court envoy Zhang Hong was ordered against rebel Qiang at Yunwu; his force was shattered and he fell in battle.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月，遣中郎將竇固監捕虜將軍馬武等二將軍討燒當羌。

translations.literal: In winter, in the eleventh month, the general of the gentlemen of the household Dou Gu was sent to supervise the general who captures barbarians Ma Wu and two other generals in a campaign against the Shao Dang Qiang.
translations.idiomatic: In winter, the eleventh month, Dou Gu of the gentlemen of the household was placed in command of Ma Wu, general who captures barbarians, and two other generals to crush the Shao Dang Qiang.

sentences.zh: 十二月甲寅，詔曰：『方春戒節，人以耕桑。

translations.literal: On the day jiayin in the twelfth month, an edict stated: "When spring brings the season of restraint, people turn to plowing and sericulture.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin in the twelfth month an edict ran: "With spring comes the season of restraint; the people should turn to tillage and silkworms.

sentences.zh: 其勑有司務順時氣，使無煩擾。

translations.literal: Order the responsible officials by all means to comply with seasonal qi and cause no needless vexation.
translations.idiomatic: Let officials honor the seasons in their orders and give the people no needless harassment.

sentences.zh: 天下亡命殊死以下，聽得贖論：死罪入縑二十匹，右趾至髡鉗城旦舂十匹，完城旦舂至司寇作三匹。

translations.literal: Those fugitives from the law, down from those liable for execution for special crimes, may hear ransom terms: for capital crimes, twenty bolts of fine silk; for right-toe amputation down through shaved-head wall-dawn labor, ten bolts; for intact wall-dawn labor down to minister-of-crimes labor, three bolts.
translations.idiomatic: Fugitives facing death or lesser capital charges may redeem themselves: twenty bolts of silk for the death sentence; ten for mutilation down to shaved-head hard labor; three for lesser corvée sentences.

sentences.zh: 其未發覺，詔書到先自告者，半入贖。

translations.literal: Those not yet discovered who, before the edict arrives, confess themselves first pay half the ransom.
translations.idiomatic: Those who surrender before discovery, once the edict reaches them, pay only half the standard ransom.

sentences.zh: 今選舉不實，邪佞未去，權門請托，殘吏放手，百姓愁怨，情無告訴。

translations.literal: At present recommendations and selections are not truthful; the wicked and flattering have not been removed; powerful families importune with requests; cruel clerks act recklessly; the hundred surnames harbor sorrow and resentment and have nowhere to lodge complaints.
translations.idiomatic: Recommendations are dishonest, sycophants remain in office, great houses peddle influence, harsh subordinates prey on the weak, and ordinary folk nurse grievances with no redress.

sentences.zh: 有司明奏罪名，幷正舉者。

translations.literal: Let the responsible officials clearly memorialize the crimes and together rectify the recommenders.
translations.idiomatic: Let officials report offenses plainly and hold recommenders accountable as well.

sentences.zh: 又郡縣每因徵發，輕為奸利，詭責羸弱，先急下貧。

translations.literal: Moreover, commanderies and counties, whenever they issue levies, lightly commit wrongful profit, falsely imposing burdens on the weak and exhausted, pressing first on the poorest below.
translations.idiomatic: When counties and commanderies call up labor or taxes, they often line their pockets, shifting costs onto the feeble and squeezing the destitute hardest.

sentences.zh: 其務在均平，無令枉刻。』

translations.literal: Their task lies in even fairness; let there be no wrongful harshness."
translations.idiomatic: Their duty is equity; let there be no arbitrary cruelty."

sentences.zh: 永平元年春正月，帝率公卿已下朝於原陵，如元會儀。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongping, in spring, in the first month, the emperor led the dukes and ministers downward in attendance at Yuanling, according to the ceremony of the yuan assembly.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 1, first month of spring: the emperor led the high ministers to Yuanling for a court observance modeled on the New Year audience.

sentences.zh: 夏五月，太傅鄧禹薨。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fifth month, the grand tutor Deng Yu died.
translations.idiomatic: In summer, the fifth month, Grand Tutor Deng Yu died.

sentences.zh: 戊寅，東海王彊薨，遣司空馮魴持節視喪事，賜升龍旄頭、鑾輅，龍旂。

translations.literal: On the day wuyin, the king of Donghai, Qiang, died; the minister of works Feng Fang was sent with credentials to oversee the funeral and was granted the ascending-dragon mao tou, the imperial carriage, and dragon banners.
translations.idiomatic: On wuyin the king of Donghai, Liu Qiang, died; Minister of Works Feng Fang went with imperial credentials to supervise the rites and received for the obsequies the highest emblems—plumed hearse escort, bell-carriage, and dragon standards.

sentences.zh: 六月乙卯，葬東海恭王。

translations.literal: On the day yimao in the sixth month, the respectful king of Donghai was buried.
translations.idiomatic: On yimao in the sixth month the late King Gong of Donghai was laid to rest.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，捕虜將軍馬武等與燒當羌戰，大破之。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the seventh month, the general who captures barbarians Ma Wu and others fought the Shao Dang Qiang and utterly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, the seventh month, Ma Wu and allied generals smashed the Shao Dang Qiang.

sentences.zh: 募士卒戍隴右，賜錢人三萬。

translations.literal: Soldiers were recruited to garrison the Long right; each man was granted thirty thousand cash.
translations.idiomatic: Recruits were sent to hold the Longxi frontier, each paid thirty thousand cash.

sentences.zh: 八月戊子，徙山陽王荊為廣陵王，遣就國。

translations.literal: On the day wuzi in the eighth month, the king of Shanyang, Jing, was moved to be king of Guangling and was sent to his state.
translations.idiomatic: On wuzi in the eighth month Prince Liu Jing of Shanyang was transferred to Guangling and ordered to his fief.

sentences.zh: 是歲，遼東太守祭肜使鮮卑擊赤山烏桓，大破之，斬其渠帥。

translations.literal: That year, the governor of Liaodong, Ji Yong, sent the Xianbei to strike the Wuhuan of Chishan; they greatly defeated them and beheaded their chieftain.
translations.idiomatic: The same year Liaodong governor Ji Yong used Xianbei allies against the Chishan Wuhuan, routed them, and executed their leader.

sentences.zh: 越巂姑復夷叛，州郡討平之。

translations.literal: The Gufu Yi of Yuexi rebelled; the province and commandery attacked and pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: The Gufu tribe at Yuexi rose; provincial forces put down the revolt.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月辛未，宗祀光武皇帝於明堂，帝及公卿列侯始服冠冕、衣裳、玉佩、絇屨以行事。

translations.literal: In the second year, in spring, in the first month, on the day xinwei, the court performed the suburban sacrifice to Emperor Guangwu in the Bright Hall; the emperor and the dukes, ministers, and ranked marquels for the first time wore caps, robes, jade girdle-pendants, and silk shoes with twisted cords in conducting the rites.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 2, xinwei in the first month of spring: Guangwu was worshipped at the Bright Hall; emperor and nobles wore full court regalia—caps, gowns, jade pendants, and formal shoes—for the first time at such a rite.

sentences.zh: 禮畢，登靈臺。

translations.literal: When the ceremony was finished, they ascended the Spirit Terrace.
translations.idiomatic: When the service ended, they climbed the Spirit Terrace.

sentences.zh: 使尚書令持節詔驃騎將軍、三公曰：『今令月吉日，宗祀光武皇帝於明堂，以配五帝。

translations.literal: The supervisor of the masters of writing was ordered with credentials to proclaim to the general-in-chief of agile cavalry and the three dukes: "On this propitious day of the proper month, the suburban sacrifice to Emperor Guangwu has been performed in the Bright Hall, with the Five Thearchs as correlates.
translations.idiomatic: The overseer of the masters of writing, bearing credentials, instructed the general-in-chief of agile cavalry and the three dukes: "On this auspicious day of an ordered month we have sacrificed to Emperor Guangwu in the Bright Hall, pairing him with the Five Thearchs.

sentences.zh: 禮備法物，樂和八音，詠祉福，舞功德，班時令，勑群后。

translations.literal: The ritual paraphernalia and regalia were complete; the music harmonized the eight tones; they chanted blessings, danced the merits, promulgated the seasonal commands, and admonished the host of lords.
translations.idiomatic: Every ritual instrument was in place; the eight kinds of instruments sounded in harmony; hymns praised Heaven's grace, dances celebrated virtue, the seasons were proclaimed, and the feudatories were charged to obey.

sentences.zh: 事畢，升靈臺，望元氣，吹時律，觀物變。

translations.literal: When the affair was concluded, they ascended the Spirit Terrace, gazed upon the primordial qi, sounded the pitch-pipes of the seasons, and observed the transformations of things.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward they mounted the Spirit Terrace to read the clouds, sound the seasonal pitch-pipes, and scan portents in nature.

sentences.zh: 群僚藩輔，宗室子孫，眾郡奉計，百蠻貢職，烏桓、濊貊咸來助祭，單于侍子、骨都侯亦皆陪位。

translations.literal: The host of officials, the feudatories as assistants, imperial kinsmen and grandsons, the multitude of commanderies presenting accounts, the hundred barbarians rendering duty, Wuhuan and Hui Mo all came to assist at the sacrifice; the Shanyu's hostage son and the bone-cap marquels likewise all attended in supporting positions.
translations.idiomatic: The full bureaucracy, imperial kin, tribute-bearing commanderies, and frontier peoples—including Wuhuan and Hui Mo—joined the rites; even the Xiongnu heir kept at court and the bone-cap nobles stood in the secondary ranks.

sentences.zh: 斯固聖祖功德之所致也。

translations.literal: This is indeed what the sage ancestor's merit and virtue have brought about.
translations.idiomatic: All of this flows from the merit of our sage forebear.

sentences.zh: 朕以闇陋，奉承大業，親執珪璧，恭祀天地。

translations.literal: I, in darkness and coarseness, receive the great enterprise; personally I hold the jade tablets and reverently sacrifice to Heaven and earth.
translations.idiomatic: Dark and unworthy as I am, I have inherited this charge; I held the jade tokens myself and bowed before Heaven and earth.

sentences.zh: 仰惟先帝受命中興，撥亂反正，以寧天下，封泰山，建明堂，立辟雍，起靈臺，恢弘大道，被之八極；

translations.literal: Looking up I reflect that the late emperor received the Mandate and revived the house, set chaos aright and returned to the correct norm, thereby bringing tranquility to the empire; he ascended Mount Tai, built the Bright Hall, established the Hong Academy, raised the Spirit Terrace, enlarged the great way, and spread it to the eight extremities;
translations.idiomatic: I recall how the late emperor received the mandate, turned rebellion into order, and gave the realm peace—how he offered the Feng sacrifice on Mount Tai, built the Bright Hall, founded the Hong gate school, raised the Spirit Terrace, and extended the Way to the ends of the earth;

sentences.zh: 而胤子無成、康之質，群臣無呂、旦之謀，盥洗進爵，踧踖惟慙。

translations.literal: yet the heir sons lack the substance of Cheng and Kang, and the host of ministers lack the plans of [the Duke of] Lu and [the Duke of] Dan; washing hands and advancing the goblet, I am cramped with shame alone.
translations.idiomatic: yet I am no young Cheng or Kang, and my ministers are no Duke of Zhou or Duke of Shao; when I rinse my hands and raise the cup, shame knots my heart.

sentences.zh: 素性頑鄙，臨事益懼，故「君子坦蕩蕩，小人長戚戚」。

translations.literal: By nature I am stubborn and rustic; facing affairs I only grow more fearful—hence "the gentleman is calm and broad; the petty man is always anxious."
translations.idiomatic: By nature I am blunt and rustic, and responsibility only deepens my fear—so the Classic says the gentleman stays open-hearted while the small man frets without end.

sentences.zh: 其令天下自殊死已下，謀反大逆，皆赦除之。

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that in the empire, from those [sentenced] to execution by dismemberment downward, for rebellion and great treason, all be pardoned and remitted.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore let every sentence short of the most gruesome death, even for rebellion and lese-majesty, be remitted throughout the realm.

sentences.zh: 百僚師尹，其勉脩厥職，順行時令，敬若昊天，以綏兆人。』

translations.literal: You, the hundred officials and the masters as shepherds, strive to cultivate your duties, comply with the seasonal commands, revere August Heaven, and thereby bring peace to the myriad people."
translations.idiomatic: Ministers and regional shepherds: perfect your offices, heed the seasons, reverence High Heaven, and so give rest to the people."

sentences.zh: 三月，臨辟雍，初行大射禮。

translations.literal: In the third month, he attended the Hong Academy and for the first time performed the great archery rite.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month he visited the Hong Academy and held the great archery ceremony for the first time.

sentences.zh: 秋九月，沛王輔、楚王英、濟南王康、淮陽王延、東海王政來朝。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the ninth month, the kings of Pei, Fu, of Chu, Ying, of Jinan, Kang, of Huaiyang, Yan, of Donghai, Zheng, came to court.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, the ninth month, the kings Fu of Pei, Ying of Chu, Kang of Jinan, Yan of Huaiyang, and Zheng of Donghai arrived for audience.

sentences.zh: 冬十月壬子，幸辟雍，初行養老禮。

translations.literal: In winter, in the tenth month, on the day renzi, he favored the Hong Academy and for the first time performed the rite of caring for the aged.
translations.idiomatic: On renzi in the tenth month of winter he visited the Hong Academy and first celebrated the old-age banquet rite.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『光武皇帝建三朝之禮，而未及臨饗。

translations.literal: An edict stated: "Emperor Guangwu established the rites of the three courts but did not reach the point of personally attending the feast.
translations.idiomatic: An edict declared: "Emperor Guangwu ordained the triple-court ritual but never lived to preside at the feast himself.

sentences.zh: 眇眇小子，屬當聖業。

translations.literal: I, the tiny minor, have been entrusted with the sacred enterprise.
translations.idiomatic: I, least of men, have been charged with that same sacred work.

sentences.zh: 間暮春吉辰，初行大射；

translations.literal: In the interval, on an auspicious day in late spring, I first performed the great archery;
translations.idiomatic: On a chosen day in late spring I first held the great archery;

sentences.zh: 令月元日，復踐辟雍。

translations.literal: on the first day of the proper month, I again trod the Hong Academy.
translations.idiomatic: then on the new moon of an ordered month I entered the Hong Academy again.

sentences.zh: 尊事三老，兄事五更，安車輭輪，供綏執授。

translations.literal: I honored the three elders as fathers and treated the five more-aged as elder brothers; soft-wheeled carriages were provided, and I offered the sash and supported them by the arm.
translations.idiomatic: I received the three elders as fathers and the five "more-aged" as elder brothers, sent cushioned carriages for them, and myself adjusted their sashes and lent an arm.

sentences.zh: 侯王設醬，公卿饌珍，朕親袒割，執爵而酳。

translations.literal: The kings and marquels set out sauces; the dukes and ministers prepared delicacies; I personally bared my shoulder for the carving and, holding the goblet, performed the rinsing rite.
translations.idiomatic: Princes set out condiments, ministers spread rare dishes, I carved the roast with bared shoulder, and poured the cleansing libation with my own hands.

sentences.zh: 祝哽在前，祝噎在後。

translations.literal: The choking-warder stood before; the swallowing-warder stood behind.
translations.idiomatic: Attendants watched lest the elders choke on the food before them or swallow amiss behind.

sentences.zh: 升歌《鹿鳴》，下管《新宮》，八佾具修，萬舞於庭。

translations.literal: Ascendant song was Deer Call; downward pipes played New Palace; the eight rows of dancers were fully arrayed, and the myriad dancers performed in the courtyard.
translations.idiomatic: Musicians sang "Deer Call" from the gallery and played "New Palace" below; the eight files of dancers moved in the full wan dance in the court.

sentences.zh: 朕固薄德，何以克當？

translations.literal: I am indeed of slight virtue; how could I be worthy to match it?"
translations.idiomatic: My virtue is thin—how dare I think myself worthy of such honor?"

sentences.zh: 《易》陳負乘，《詩》刺彼己，永念慙疚，無忘厥心。

translations.literal: The Changes sets forth "riding [when one should] walk"; the Odes satirizes "them and themselves"; forever I ponder shame and fault and do not forget it in my heart.
translations.idiomatic: The Book of Changes warns against riding when one should walk; the Odes mock those who overreach themselves; I carry that reproach in my heart and cannot forget it.

sentences.zh: 三老李躬，年耆學明。

translations.literal: The third elder, Li Gong, is advanced in years and clear in learning.
translations.idiomatic: Third Elder Li Gong is venerable in years and eminent in scholarship.

sentences.zh: 五更桓榮，授朕《尚書》。

translations.literal: The fifth more-aged, Huan Rong, imparted to me the Documents.
translations.idiomatic: Fifth More-Aged Huan Rong taught me the Book of Documents.

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰：「無德不報，無言不酬。

translations.literal: The Odes says, "There is no virtue that goes unrequited, no word that goes unanswered.
translations.idiomatic: The Odes say, "No kindness lacks its reward, no word lacks its reply.

sentences.zh: 」其賜榮爵關內侯，食邑五千戶。

translations.literal: " Let there be granted to Rong the noble rank of marquis within the passes, with a fief income of five thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: " Grant Huan Rong rank as marquis within the passes with income from five thousand households.

sentences.zh: 三老、五更皆以二千石祿養終厥身。

translations.literal: The third elders and five more-aged shall all be maintained at two thousand piculs salary to the end of their lives.
translations.idiomatic: The three elders and five more-aged shall each draw a two-thousand-picul stipend for life.

sentences.zh: 其賜天下三老酒人一石，肉四十斤。

translations.literal: Let there be granted to the three elders of the empire one shi of wine per person and forty jin of meat.
translations.idiomatic: Let every village elder in the realm receive a stone of wine and forty pounds of meat.

sentences.zh: 有司其存耆耋，恤幼孤，惠鰥寡，稱朕意焉。』

translations.literal: Let the responsible officials cherish the aged and extremely old, pity the young and orphaned, show kindness to widowers and widows, and thereby accord with my intent."
translations.idiomatic: Let officials care for the very old, succor orphans, and comfort the widowed, that they may reflect my will."

sentences.zh: 中山王焉始就國。

translations.literal: The king of Zhongshan, Yan, for the first time went to his state.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Zhongshan, Liu Yan, went to his fief for the first time.

sentences.zh: 甲子，西巡狩，幸長安，祠高廟，遂有事於十一陵。

translations.literal: On the day jiazi, he toured west on a hunt, favored Chang'an, sacrificed at the lofty temple, then performed the full rites at the eleven mausolea.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi he began a western progress, visited Chang'an, offered sacrifice at Gaozu's temple, and worshipped at all eleven Western Han tombs.

sentences.zh: 歷覽館邑，會郡縣吏，勞賜作樂。

translations.literal: He passed through and inspected the lodges and cities, assembled the commandery and county officials, consoled them with gifts, and had music performed.
translations.idiomatic: He inspected the towns along the route, called in local officials, feasted them, and had music played.

sentences.zh: 十一月甲申，遣使者以中牢祠蕭何、霍光。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, on the day jiashen, envoys were sent with the middle victim to sacrifice to Xiao He and Huo Guang.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month, on jiashen, envoys sacrificed with an ox-and-sheep offering at the shrines of Xiao He and Huo Guang.

sentences.zh: 帝謁陵園，過式其墓。

translations.literal: The emperor visited the tomb parks and, passing by, performed the "pattern" rite at their graves.
translations.idiomatic: Touring the mausoleum precincts, he halted his carriage before their tombs in the prescribed show of respect.

sentences.zh: 進幸河東，所過賜二千石、令長已下至於掾史，各有差。

translations.literal: He advanced and favored Hedong; wherever he passed he granted gifts to those of two thousand piculs, magistrates and chiefs downward to clerks and secretaries, each according to a graded scale.
translations.idiomatic: He went on into Hedong, bestowing gifts on every official from two-thousand-picul rank down to clerks, each according to grade.

sentences.zh: 癸卯，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On the day guimao, the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On guimao the court returned to the capital.

sentences.zh: 十二月，護羌校尉竇林下獄死。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, the colonel director of the Qiang, Dou Lin, entered prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Dou Lin, colonel director of the Qiang, was imprisoned and died there.

sentences.zh: 是歲，始迎氣於五郊。

translations.literal: That year, for the first time the welcoming of seasonal qi was performed at the five suburban altars.
translations.idiomatic: That year the court first welcomed the qi of each season at the five suburban stations.

sentences.zh: 少府陰就子豐殺其妻酈邑公主，就坐自殺。

translations.literal: The son of the privy treasurer Yin Jiu, Feng, killed his wife, the princess of Liyi; Jiu thereupon committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: Yin Feng, son of privy treasurer Yin Jiu, murdered his wife, Princess Liyi; his father then took his own life.

sentences.zh: 三年春正月癸巳，詔曰：『朕奉郊祀，登靈臺，見史官，正儀度。

translations.literal: In the third year, in spring, in the first month, on the day guisi, an edict stated: "I have attended the suburban sacrifice, ascended the Spirit Terrace, seen the clerks-historians, and corrected the instruments and measures.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 3, guisi in the first month of spring: an edict said, "After the suburban rites I climbed the Spirit Terrace, consulted the court astronomers, and aligned the calendar and instruments.

sentences.zh: 夫春者，歲之始也。

translations.literal: Spring is the beginning of the year.
translations.idiomatic: Spring opens the year.

sentences.zh: 始得其正，則三時有成。

translations.literal: If the beginning obtains correctness, then the three seasons have accomplishment.
translations.idiomatic: If the start is true, the other three seasons will prosper.

sentences.zh: 比者水旱不節，邊人食寡，政失於上，人受其咎，有司其勉順時氣，勸督農桑，去其螟蜮，以及蝥賊；

translations.literal: Recently flood and drought have not kept to their seasons; the border people lack food; policy has failed above, and the people below receive the blame; let the responsible officials strive to comply with seasonal qi, urge and supervise farming and sericulture, remove the grain-eating caterpillars, and extend this to the field mice and sparrows;
translations.idiomatic: Lately floods and droughts have broken their seasons; frontier folk go hungry; bad government above brings suffering below—let officials harmonize their orders with the seasons, push plowing and silkworms, clear away insect pests and vermin from the fields;

sentences.zh: 詳刑慎罰，明察單辭，夙夜匪懈，以稱朕意。』

translations.literal: examine punishments carefully, clarify and inspect single-sided testimony, be untiring morning and night, and thereby accord with my intent."
translations.idiomatic: review sentences with care, weigh uncorroborated testimony, toil early and late, and so match my purpose."

sentences.zh: 二月甲寅，太尉趙憙、司徒李訢免。

translations.literal: In the second month, on the day jiayin, the grand commandant Zhao Xi and the minister of education Li Xin were dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin in the second month Grand Commandant Zhao Xi and Minister of Education Li Xin were removed from office.

sentences.zh: 丙辰，左馮翊郭丹為司徒。

translations.literal: On the day bingchen, Guo Dan, colonel of internal affairs for Zuo Fengyi, became minister of education.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen Guo Dan, governor of Zuo Fengyi, was appointed minister of education.

sentences.zh: 己未，南陽太守虞延為太尉。

translations.literal: On the day jiwei, Yu Yan, governor of Nanyang, became grand commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei Yu Yan, governor of Nanyang, was made grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 甲子，立貴人馬氏為皇后，皇子炟為皇太子。

translations.literal: On the day jiazi, the honorable lady Ma was established as empress, and the imperial son Da as heir apparent.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi Lady Ma was raised to empress and Prince Da named crown prince.

sentences.zh: 賜天下男子爵，人二級；

translations.literal: Let there be granted to the men of the empire noble ranks, two grades per person;
translations.idiomatic: Every man in the realm received two steps in noble rank;

sentences.zh: 三老、孝悌、力田人三級；

translations.literal: to the village elders, the exemplars of filial piety and fraternal duty, and the champions of diligent farming, three grades per person;
translations.idiomatic: three steps each for village elders, recognized paragons of filial piety and brotherly love, and outstanding farmers;

sentences.zh: 流人無名數欲占者人一級；

translations.literal: to wandering people without registered names who wish to settle their registration, one grade per person;
translations.idiomatic: one step for any unregistered vagrant who wished to take up legal residence;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃、貧不能自存者粟，人五斛。

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, the solitary, the seriously ill, and the poor unable to support themselves, grain, five hu per person.
translations.idiomatic: five hu of grain for each widower, widow, orphan, person alone, the gravely disabled, and the destitute who could not live by their own means.

sentences.zh: 夏四月辛酉，封皇子建為千乘王，羨為廣平王。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fourth month, on the day xinyou, the imperial sons Jian were enfeoffed as king of Qiansheng and Xian as king of Guangping.
translations.idiomatic: On xinyou in the fourth month of summer Prince Jian became King of Qiansheng and Prince Xian King of Guangping.

sentences.zh: 六月丁卯，有星孛於天船北。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, on the day dingmao, there was a broom star north of Celestial Boat.
translations.idiomatic: On dingmao in the sixth month a comet appeared north of the Celestial Boat asterism.

sentences.zh: 秋八月戊辰，改大樂為大予樂。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the eighth month, on the day wuchen, Great Music was renamed Great Grant Music.
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen in the eighth month of autumn the court renamed the imperial music bureau from "Great Music" to "Great Grant Music."

sentences.zh: 壬申晦，日有蝕之。

translations.literal: On the last day of the month renshen, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: At month's end, on renshen, the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『朕奉承祖業，無有善政。

translations.literal: An edict stated: "I receive and uphold the ancestral enterprise, yet there is no good government.
translations.idiomatic: An edict read: "I inherit the work of my ancestors, yet my rule has brought little good.

sentences.zh: 日月薄蝕，彗孛見天，水旱不節，稼穡不成，人無宿儲，下生愁墊。

translations.literal: Sun and moon have thinned and been eclipsed; comets and broom stars have appeared in the sky; flood and drought have not kept to their seasons; sowing and reaping have not succeeded; people lack stored provisions; those below are sunk in sorrow and distress.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven shows eclipses of sun and moon; comets streak the sky; rains and droughts ignore the seasons; the harvests fail; households have no reserves; the people groan under hardship.

sentences.zh: 雖夙夜勤思，而智能不逮。

translations.literal: Although morning and night I labor in thought, my wisdom and ability do not reach far enough.
translations.idiomatic: Though I think on it night and day, my wit is not equal to the task.

sentences.zh: 昔楚莊無災，以致戒懼；

translations.literal: In olden times King Zhuang of Chu had no calamity, yet thereby grew vigilant and fearful;
translations.idiomatic: King Zhuang of Chu needed no disaster to teach him fear;

sentences.zh: 魯哀禍大，天不降譴。

translations.literal: Duke Ai of Lu suffered great misfortune, yet Heaven sent down no reproof.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Ai of Lu knew catastrophe, yet Heaven withheld its rebuke.

sentences.zh: 今之動變，儻尚可救。

translations.literal: As for the present movements and changes, perhaps they may still be remedied.
translations.idiomatic: These portents we see today may yet be answered if we mend our ways.

sentences.zh: 有司勉思厥職，以匡無德。

translations.literal: Let the responsible officials strive to ponder their duties, in order to assist my lack of virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Let every office search its conscience and help redeem my want of virtue.

sentences.zh: 古者卿士獻詩，百工箴諫。

translations.literal: In antiquity ministers presented odes, and the hundred crafts offered admonitions.
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity high ministers brought poems to court and artisans spoke blunt truth.

sentences.zh: 其言事者，靡有所諱。』

translations.literal: As for those who speak on affairs of state, let there be nothing they must hide."
translations.idiomatic: Whoever memorializes on policy shall not fear to speak the whole truth."

sentences.zh: 冬十月，蒸祭光武廟，初奏《文始》、《五行》、《武德》之舞。

translations.literal: In winter, in the tenth month, the winter sacrifice was performed at Emperor Guangwu's temple; for the first time the dances of 《Wen Shi》, 《Wu Xing》, and 《Wu De》 were performed.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter the winter offering was made at Guangwu's temple, and for the first time the court danced the suites 《Wen Shi》, 《Wu Xing》, and 《Wu De》.

sentences.zh: 甲子，車駕從皇太后幸章陵，觀舊廬。

translations.literal: On the day jiazi, the imperial carriage followed the empress dowager in favoring Zhangling and viewing the old dwellings.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi the emperor accompanied the empress dowager to Zhangling to visit her childhood home.

sentences.zh: 十二月戊辰，至自章陵。

translations.literal: On the day wuchen in the twelfth month, he arrived, coming from Zhangling.
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen in the twelfth month he returned from Zhangling.

sentences.zh: 是歲，起北宮及諸官府。

translations.literal: That year, construction was begun on the Northern Palace and the various government bureaus.
translations.idiomatic: That year work began on the Northern Palace and the new ministry compounds.

sentences.zh: 京師及郡國七大水。

translations.literal: The capital region and the commanderies and principalities suffered seven great floods.
translations.idiomatic: Seven great floods struck the capital and the provinces.

sentences.zh: 四年春二月辛亥，詔曰：『朕親耕藉田，以祈農事。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, in spring, in the second month, on the day xinhai, an edict stated: "I have personally plowed the sacred field in order to pray for the farming enterprise.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 4, xinhai in the second month of spring: an edict said, "I have turned the furrow in the sacred field myself to pray for the crops.

sentences.zh: 京師冬無宿雪，春不燠沐，煩勞群司，積精禱求。

translations.literal: In the capital through winter there was no overnight snow; in spring there was no warm soaking rain; we have troubled the host of offices and gathered earnest spirit in prayer and seeking.
translations.idiomatic: Through the winter the capital saw no lasting snow; spring brought no steady warm rains; I have wearied the bureaucracy with repeated intercessory rites.

sentences.zh: 而比再得時雨，宿麥潤澤。

translations.literal: Yet recently twice timely rains have been obtained, and the winter wheat has been moistened and enriched.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Heaven has twice sent seasonable rain, and the winter wheat has drunk deep.

sentences.zh: 其賜公卿半奉。

translations.literal: Let there be granted to the dukes and ministers half their salaries.
translations.idiomatic: Let the dukes and ministers receive half their normal stipends as a token of thanks.

sentences.zh: 有司勉遵時政，務平刑罰。』

translations.literal: Let the responsible officials strive to comply with the policies of the season and by all means keep punishments even."
translations.idiomatic: Let officials align their work with the seasons and keep justice even-handed."

sentences.zh: 秋九月戊寅，千乘王建薨。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the ninth month, on the day wuyin, the king of Qiansheng, Jian, died.
translations.idiomatic: On wuyin in the ninth month of autumn King Jian of Qiansheng died.

sentences.zh: 冬十月乙卯，司徒郭丹、司空馮魴免。

translations.literal: In winter, in the tenth month, on the day yimao, the minister of education Guo Dan and the minister of works Feng Fang were dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On yimao in the tenth month of winter Minister of Education Guo Dan and Minister of Works Feng Fang were dismissed.

sentences.zh: 丙辰，河南尹范遷為司徒，太僕伏恭為司空。

translations.literal: On the day bingchen, Fan Qian, governor of Henan, became minister of education, and Fu Gong, grand coachman, became minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen Fan Qian of Henan became minister of education and Grand Coachman Fu Gong minister of works.

sentences.zh: 十二月，陵鄉侯梁松下獄死。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, Marquis of Lingxiang Liang Song entered prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Liang Song, Marquis of Lingxiang, was imprisoned and died there.

sentences.zh: 五年春二月庚戌，驃騎將軍東平王蒼罷歸藩；

translations.literal: In the fifth year, in spring, in the second month, on the day gengxu, the general-in-chief of agile cavalry, the king of Dongping, Cang, was dismissed and returned to his fief;
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 5, gengxu in the second month of spring: Liu Cang, king of Dongping, was relieved as general-in-chief of agile cavalry and sent back to his kingdom;

sentences.zh: 琅邪王京就國。

translations.literal: the king of Langye, Jing, went to his state.
translations.idiomatic: the king of Langye, Liu Jing, departed for his fief.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，行幸鄴。

translations.literal: In winter, in the tenth month, he traveled in favor to Ye.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter he traveled to Ye.

sentences.zh: 與趙王栩會鄴。

translations.literal: He met the king of Zhao, Xu, at Ye.
translations.idiomatic: There he joined the king of Zhao, Liu Xu.

sentences.zh: 常山三老言於帝曰：『上生於元氏，願蒙優復。

translations.literal: The village elder of Changshan spoke to the emperor, saying: "The Sovereign was born in Yuanshi; we beg to receive favorable remission."
translations.idiomatic: A Changshan village elder told the emperor, "Your Majesty was born in Yuanshi; we ask a special favor for that place."

sentences.zh: 』詔曰：『豐、沛、濟陽，受命所由，加恩報德，適其宜也。

translations.literal: " An edict stated: "Feng, Pei, and Jiyang are where the Mandate was received; adding favor and repaying virtue is fitting in their case.
translations.idiomatic: The court replied: "Feng, Pei, and Jiyang are where the dynasty first received Heaven's mandate; it is right to heap favors on them.

sentences.zh: 今永平之政，百姓怨結，而吏人求復，令人愧笑。

translations.literal: Now under the government of Yongping the hundred surnames are bound in resentment, yet clerks and people seek remission—this makes one ashamed and inclined to laugh.
translations.idiomatic: Yet under my Yongping rule the people nurse grievance, while local clerks still ask for tax holidays—an embarrassment that invites ridicule.

sentences.zh: 重逆此縣之拳拳，其復元氏縣田租更賦六歲，勞賜縣掾史，及門闌走卒。

translations.literal: Weighing heavily this county's earnest sincerity, let Yuanshi county's field rent and rotating labor tax be remitted for six years; console with gifts the county clerks and gate runners."
translations.idiomatic: Still, out of respect for Yuanshi's loyalty, remit land tax and irregular levies there for six years, and reward its clerks and gate guards."

sentences.zh: 』至自鄴。

translations.literal: " He arrived, coming from Ye.
translations.idiomatic: He then returned from Ye.

sentences.zh: 十一月，北匈奴寇五原；

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, the Northern Xiongnu raided Wuyuan;
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month the Northern Xiongnu struck Wuyuan;

sentences.zh: 十二月；

translations.literal: in the twelfth month;
translations.idiomatic: in the twelfth month

sentences.zh: 寇雲中，南單于擊卻之。

translations.literal: they raided Yunzhong; the Southern Shanyu struck and drove them back.
translations.idiomatic: they raided Yunzhong until the Southern Shanyu counterattacked and threw them back.

sentences.zh: 是歲，發遣邊人在內郡者，賜裝錢人二萬。

translations.literal: That year, border people who had been settled in inner commanderies were sent off and granted traveling money of twenty thousand cash per person.
translations.idiomatic: That year frontier folk who had been moved inland were sent home with twenty thousand cash each for expenses.

sentences.zh: 六年春正月，沛王輔、楚王英、東平王蒼、淮陽王延、浪邪王京、東海王政、趙王盱、北海王興、齊王石來朝。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, in spring, in the first month, the kings Fu of Pei, Ying of Chu, Cang of Dongping, Yan of Huaiyang, Jing of Langye, Zheng of Donghai, Xu of Zhao, Xing of Beihai, and Shi of Qi came to court.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 6, first month of spring: the kings Fu of Pei, Ying of Chu, Cang of Dongping, Yan of Huaiyang, Jing of Langye, Zheng of Donghai, Xu of Zhao, Xing of Beihai, and Shi of Qi all attended court.

sentences.zh: 二月，王雒山出寶鼎，廬江太守獻之。

translations.literal: In the second month, at Mount Wangluo a precious tripod emerged, and the governor of Lujiang presented it.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month a ritual tripod surfaced on Mount Wangluo; the governor of Lujiang sent it to the throne.

sentences.zh: 夏四月甲子，詔曰：『昔禹收九牧之金，鑄鼎以象物，使人知神姦，不逢惡氣。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fourth month, on the day jiazi, an edict stated: "In olden times Yu gathered metal from the nine shepherds, cast tripods to image the myriad things, caused people to know the spirits and the treacherous, and not encounter evil qi.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi in the fourth month of summer an edict said: "Yu once took bronze from the nine provinces and cast tripods to represent all creatures, so men could tell spirit from fraud and avoid malignant influences.

sentences.zh: 遭德則興，遷於商、周；

translations.literal: When he encountered virtue, they rose and moved to Shang and Zhou;
translations.idiomatic: Under true virtue they flourished and passed to Shang and Zhou;

sentences.zh: 周德既衰，鼎乃淪亡。

translations.literal: when Zhou's virtue had declined, the tripods then sank and were lost.
translations.idiomatic: when Zhou's power waned, they sank into oblivion.

sentences.zh: 祥瑞之降，以應有德。

translations.literal: The descent of auspicious omens is to answer to those possessed of virtue.
translations.idiomatic: Good omens appear only to answer virtuous rule.

sentences.zh: 方今政化多僻，何以致茲？

translations.literal: At present government and transformation are mostly deviant—by what means have we brought this about?"
translations.idiomatic: Today policy is skewed in many ways—what have we done to deserve such a sign?"

sentences.zh: 《易》曰鼎象三公，豈公卿奉職得其理邪？

translations.literal: The 《Changes》 says the tripod images the three dukes—can it be that the dukes and ministers in performing their duties have obtained the right pattern?"
translations.idiomatic: The 《Book of Changes》 likens the tripod to the three dukes—are my highest ministers failing in their charge?"

sentences.zh: 太常其以礿祭之日，陳鼎於廟，以備器用。

translations.literal: Let the chamberlain for ceremonials on the day of the yue sacrifice display the tripod in the temple, to complete the ritual vessels.
translations.idiomatic: Let the minister of rituals set the tripod in the ancestral temple on the day of the spring offering, among the full set of vessels.

sentences.zh: 賜三公帛五十匹，九卿、二千石半之。

translations.literal: Let there be granted to the three dukes fifty bolts of silk each, and to the nine ministers and those of two thousand piculs half that amount.
translations.idiomatic: Award the three dukes fifty bolts of silk each and the nine ministers and two-thousand-picul officials half as much.

sentences.zh: 先帝詔書，禁人上事言聖，而間者章奏頗多浮詞，自今若有過稱虛譽，尚書皆宜抑而不省，示不為諂子蚩也。』

translations.literal: The late emperor's edict forbade people in memorials to use the word "sage," yet recently petitions have contained many empty phrases; from this time, if there are excessive praises and hollow reputations, the masters of writing should all suppress them and not examine them through, showing that we do not indulge flatterers."
translations.idiomatic: My father's edict barred memorials from calling anyone "sage," yet petitions lately brim with hollow praise; henceforth the masters of writing shall file away all such sycophancy unread, to show that the court will not humor toadies."

sentences.zh: 冬十月，行幸魯，祠東海恭王陵；

translations.literal: In winter, in the tenth month, he traveled in favor to Lu and sacrificed at the tomb of the respectful king of Donghai;
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter he journeyed to Lu and offered sacrifice at the tomb of the late King Gong of Donghai;

sentences.zh: 會沛王輔、楚王英、濟南王康、東平王蒼、淮陽王延、琅邪王京、東海王政。

translations.literal: he met the kings Fu of Pei, Ying of Chu, Kang of Jinan, Cang of Dongping, Yan of Huaiyang, Jing of Langye, and Zheng of Donghai.
translations.idiomatic: there he was joined by the kings Fu of Pei, Ying of Chu, Kang of Jinan, Cang of Dongping, Yan of Huaiyang, Jing of Langye, and Zheng of Donghai.

sentences.zh: 十二月，還，幸陽城，遣使者祠中嶽。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, returning, he favored Yangcheng and sent envoys to sacrifice at the central marchmount.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month, on the return journey, he stopped at Yangcheng and dispatched envoys to worship the sacred peak of the center.

sentences.zh: 壬午，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On the day renwu, the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu the court was back in the capital.

sentences.zh: 東平王蒼、琅邪王京從駕來朝皇太后。

translations.literal: The king of Dongping, Cang, and the king of Langye, Jing, followed the carriage and came to court the empress dowager.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Cang of Dongping and Liu Jing of Langye had accompanied the tour and now attended the empress dowager.

sentences.zh: 七年春正月癸卯，皇太后陰氏崩。

translations.literal: In the seventh year, in spring, in the first month, on the day guimao, Empress Dowager Yin died.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 7, guimao in the first month of spring: Empress Dowager Yin died.

sentences.zh: 二月庚申，葬光烈皇后。

translations.literal: On the day gengshen in the second month, Empress Guanglie was buried.
translations.idiomatic: On gengshen in the second month she was buried with the posthumous title Empress Guanglie.

sentences.zh: 秋八月戊辰，北海王興薨。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the eighth month, on the day wuchen, the king of Beihai, Xing, died.
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen in the eighth month of autumn King Xing of Beihai died.

sentences.zh: 是歲，北匈奴遣使乞和親。

translations.literal: That year, the Northern Xiongnu sent envoys begging for peace through marriage alliance.
translations.idiomatic: That year the Northern Xiongnu asked for peace and a marriage alliance.

sentences.zh: 遣越騎司馬鄭眾報使北匈奴。

translations.literal: The chief commandant of the swift riders Zheng Zhong was sent as envoy in return to the Northern Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng Zhong, chief commandant of the swift riders, was sent as ambassador to the Northern Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 初置度遼將軍，屯五原曼柏。

translations.literal: For the first time the general who crosses the Liao was established, encamped at Manbai in Wuyuan.
translations.idiomatic: The court first created the post of general who crosses the Liao, with headquarters at Manbai in Wuyuan.

sentences.zh: 秋，郡國十四雨水。

translations.literal: In autumn, fourteen commanderies and principalities had rain damage.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn fourteen provinces reported flood damage from rain.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，北宮成。

translations.literal: In winter, in the tenth month, the Northern Palace was completed.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter the Northern Palace was finished.

sentences.zh: 丙子，臨辟雍，養三老、五更。

translations.literal: On the day bingzi, he attended the Hong Academy and cared for the three elders and five more-aged.
translations.idiomatic: On bingzi he went to the Hong Academy for the old-age rites honoring the three elders and five more-aged.

sentences.zh: 禮畢，詔三公募郡國中都官死罪繫囚，減罪一等，勿笞，詣度遼將軍營，屯朔方、五原之邊縣；

translations.literal: When the ceremony was finished, he ordered the three dukes to gather from the commanderies and principalities capital prisoners under sentence of death, reduce their crimes one degree, not flog them, and send them to the camp of the general who crosses the Liao, to be stationed in the border counties of Shuofang and Wuyuan;
translations.idiomatic: After the rites he told the three dukes to collect from the provinces condemned prisoners facing death, commute their sentences one degree, spare them the rod, and march them to the general who crosses the Liao for garrison duty on the Shuofang and Wuyuan frontier;

sentences.zh: 妻子自隨，便占著邊縣；

translations.literal: wives and children might follow of their own accord and then register residence in the border counties;
translations.idiomatic: wives and children might follow and settle on the frontier;

sentences.zh: 父母同產欲相代者，恣聽之。

translations.literal: where parents or full siblings wished to substitute for one another, let them be heard as they will.
translations.idiomatic: if parents or full brothers wished to take one another's place in exile, the court would allow it.

sentences.zh: 其大逆無道殊死者，一切募下蠶室。

translations.literal: As for those guilty of great treason against the Way and liable to execution by dismemberment, in every case recruit them down to the silkworm chamber.
translations.idiomatic: Those guilty of lese-majesty and special capital crimes were to be castrated instead of executed.

sentences.zh: 亡命者令贖罪各有差。

translations.literal: Fugitives from the law were ordered to ransom their crimes, each according to a graded scale.
translations.idiomatic: Fugitives might buy off their sentences on a fixed scale.

sentences.zh: 凡徙者，賜弓弩衣粮。

translations.literal: In all cases of those transferred, bows, crossbows, clothing, and grain were granted.
translations.idiomatic: Every man sent to the border received bow, crossbow, clothing, and rations.

sentences.zh: 壬寅晦，日有食之，既。

translations.literal: On the last day of the month renyin, there was an eclipse of the sun; it was total.
translations.idiomatic: At month's end on renyin the sun was totally eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『朕以無德，奉承大業，而下貽人怨，上動三光。

translations.literal: An edict stated: "I, lacking virtue, receive the great enterprise, yet below I bequeath resentment to the people, above I move the three luminaries.
translations.idiomatic: An edict said: "Without merit I hold the throne, yet my faults stir resentment below and disturb sun, moon, and stars above.

sentences.zh: 日食之變，其災尤大，《春秋》圖讖所為至譴。

translations.literal: As for the transformation of a solar eclipse, its calamity is especially great—it is what the Spring and Autumn Annals and the prognostication charts treat as the utmost reproof.
translations.idiomatic: A total eclipse is the gravest of omens—the sort the 《Spring and Autumn Annals》 and apocryphal charts count as Heaven's sternest warning.

sentences.zh: 永思厥咎，在予一人。

translations.literal: Forever pondering where the fault lies, it rests in me alone.
translations.idiomatic: The blame rests on me alone.

sentences.zh: 群司勉修職事，極言無諱。

translations.literal: Let the host of offices strive to cultivate their official tasks and speak to the utmost without concealment."
translations.idiomatic: Let every minister perfect his duty and speak without reserve."

sentences.zh: 』於是在位者皆上封事，各言得失。

translations.literal: Thereupon those in office all submitted sealed memorials, each speaking of gain and loss.
translations.idiomatic: At once the whole bureaucracy filed sealed memorials on what was right and wrong in government.

sentences.zh: 帝覽章，深自引咎，乃以所上班示百官。

translations.literal: The emperor perused the documents, deeply took the blame upon himself, then displayed to the hundred officials what had been submitted.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor read each paper, shouldered the fault himself, and circulated the bundle to the full court.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『群僚所言，皆朕之過。

translations.literal: An edict stated: "What the host of officials have said is all my fault.
translations.idiomatic: He then issued an edict: "Every charge these men have laid is mine.

sentences.zh: 人冤不能理，吏黠不能禁；

translations.literal: The people's wrongs I have been unable to set right; crafty clerks I have been unable to restrain;
translations.idiomatic: I have left injustices unredressed and let clever clerks run wild;

sentences.zh: 而輕用人力，繕修宮宇，出入無節，喜怒過差。

translations.literal: yet I have lightly used human labor, repaired and built palaces and halls, gone out and come in without restraint, and let joy and anger exceed the proper measure.
translations.idiomatic: I have wasted labor on palace work, come and gone without measure, and let pleasure and anger overshoot decency.

sentences.zh: 昔應門失守，《關雎》刺世；

translations.literal: In olden times when the outer gate lost its guard, the 《Guan Ju》 satirized the age;
translations.idiomatic: The 《Odes》 warn that when the outer gate stands unguarded, the age is rotten;

sentences.zh: 飛蓬隨風，微子所嘆。

translations.literal: thistle down drifts with the wind—that is what the viscount of Wei sighed over.
translations.idiomatic: and thistledown blown on the wind is what the Viscount of Wei lamented.

sentences.zh: 永覽前戒，竦然兢懼。

translations.literal: Forever viewing the former warnings, I am alone in fearful trembling.
translations.idiomatic: I study those old lessons and tremble.

sentences.zh: 徒恐薄德，久而致怠耳。』

translations.literal: I only fear that, my slight virtue being prolonged, in time it will lead to negligence."
translations.idiomatic: I fear only that thin virtue, if prolonged, will slide into slackness."

sentences.zh: 北匈奴寇西河諸郡。

translations.literal: The Northern Xiongnu raided the commanderies west of the River.
translations.idiomatic: The Northern Xiongnu raided the commanderies west of the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 九年春三月辛丑，詔郡國死罪囚減罪，與妻子詣五原、朔方占著，所在死者皆賜妻父若男同產一人復終身；

translations.literal: In the ninth year, in spring, in the third month, on the day xinchou, it was ordered that capital prisoners in the commanderies and principalities sentenced to death have their crimes reduced and, with wives and children, go to Wuyuan and Shuofang to register residence; wherever among them there were deaths, in every case grant to the wife's father or one male of the same generation as the husband exemption for life;
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 9, xinchou in the third month of spring: death-row convicts in the provinces were to be commuted and resettled with their families in Wuyuan or Shuofang; if any died en route, their father-in-law or one adult clansman of the husband was excused taxes for life;

sentences.zh: 其妻無父兄獨有母者，賜其母錢六萬，又復其口筭。

translations.literal: where the wife had no father or elder brother and had only a mother, grant the mother sixty thousand cash and moreover remit her poll tax reckoning.
translations.idiomatic: if a wife had no father or brothers and only a mother survived, that mother received sixty thousand cash and exemption from the poll tax.

sentences.zh: 夏四月甲辰，詔郡國以公田賜貧人各有差。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fourth month, on the day jiachen, it was ordered that the commanderies and principalities grant public fields to the poor, each according to a graded scale.
translations.idiomatic: On jiachen in the fourth month of summer each province was told to assign public land to the needy according to need.

sentences.zh: 令司隸校尉、部刺史歲上墨綬長吏視事三歲已上理狀尤異者各一人，與計偕上。

translations.literal: It was ordered that the metropolitan commandant and the regional inspectors each year memorialize one black-ribbon senior official of three years or more in office whose administrative record was especially unusual, to come up with the annual accounts;
translations.idiomatic: The metropolitan commandant and the regional inspectors were to nominate yearly one magistrate of three years' standing whose record was outstanding, to travel with the tribute accounts;

sentences.zh: 及尤不政理者，亦以聞。

translations.literal: and as for those especially lacking in good government, likewise report them.
translations.idiomatic: and to report as well any magistrate conspicuously incompetent.

sentences.zh: 是歲，大有年。

translations.literal: That year, there was a great harvest year.
translations.idiomatic: That year the harvests were abundant.

sentences.zh: 為四姓小侯開立學校，置《五經》師。

translations.literal: For the lesser marquises of the four surnames schools were opened and masters of the 《Five Classics》 were appointed.
translations.idiomatic: The court founded schools for the four-surname lesser marquises and appointed instructors in each of the Five Classics.

sentences.zh: 十年春二月，廣陵王荊有罪，自殺，國除。

translations.literal: In the tenth year, in spring, in the second month, the king of Guangling, Jing, having committed a crime, committed suicide, and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 10, second month of spring: Liu Jing, king of Guangling, was found guilty and took his own life; his kingdom was struck from the rolls.

sentences.zh: 夏四月戊子，詔曰：『昔歲五谷登衍，今茲蠶麥善收，其大赦天下。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fourth month, on the day wuzi, an edict stated: "In past years the five grains mounted in abundance; now this year silkworms and wheat have yielded well; let there be a great amnesty of the empire.
translations.idiomatic: On wuzi in the fourth month of summer an edict declared: "Last year the five grains piled high in the granaries; this year silkworms and wheat have again prospered; I therefore grant a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 方盛夏長養之時，蕩滌宿惡，以報農功。

translations.literal: It is the season when midsummer fosters growth; wash away long-standing evil in order to repay the farming achievement.
translations.idiomatic: Midsummer is the season of growth; sweep away old guilt in return for the farmers' labor.

sentences.zh: 百姓勉務桑稼，以備災害。

translations.literal: Let the hundred surnames strive at mulberry and grain, in order to prepare against disaster.
translations.idiomatic: Let every household work silkworms and fields against famine and flood.

sentences.zh: 吏敬厥職，無令愆墮。

translations.literal: Let clerks revere their duties and not allow negligence and collapse.
translations.idiomatic: Let officials honor their posts and not grow slack.

sentences.zh: 閏月甲午，南巡狩，幸南陽，祠章陵。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month, on the day jiawu, he toured south on a hunt, favored Nanyang, and sacrificed at Zhangling.
translations.idiomatic: In the intercalary month, on jiawu, he began a southern progress, visited Nanyang, and offered sacrifice at Zhangling.

sentences.zh: 日北至，又祠舊宅。

translations.literal: When the sun reached its northernmost point, he again sacrificed at the old dwelling.
translations.idiomatic: At the summer solstice he worshipped again at the old family compound.

sentences.zh: 禮畢，召校官弟子作雅樂，奏《鹿鳴》，帝自御塤篪和之，以娛嘉賓。

translations.literal: When the ceremony was finished, he summoned the students of the school officers to perform elegant music, playing 《Lu Ming》, and the emperor himself played the xun and chi in harmony, in order to entertain the fine guests.
translations.idiomatic: When the rites ended he called the academy students to play classical music, including the piece "Lu Ming" from the Odes, while he himself played clay ocarina and bamboo chi in accompaniment for his guests.

sentences.zh: 還，幸南頓，勞饗三老、官屬。

translations.literal: Returning, he favored Nandun, consoled with gifts and feasted the three elders and official subordinates.
translations.idiomatic: On the way back he stopped at Nandun, feasted the three elders and local staff, and distributed gifts.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月，徵淮陽王延會平輿，徵沛王輔會睢陽。

translations.literal: In winter, in the eleventh month, the king of Huaiyang, Yan, was summoned to meet at Pingyu, and the king of Pei, Fu, was summoned to meet at Suiyang.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month of winter Prince Yan of Huaiyang was ordered to Pingyu and Prince Fu of Pei to Suiyang for imperial conferences.

sentences.zh: 十二月甲午，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: On the day jiawu in the twelfth month, the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On jiawu in the twelfth month the court returned to the capital.

sentences.zh: 十一年春正月，沛王輔、楚王英、濟南王康、東平王蒼、淮陽王延、中山王焉、琅邪王京、東海王政來朝。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year, in spring, in the first month, the kings Fu of Pei, Ying of Chu, Kang of Jinan, Cang of Dongping, Yan of Huaiyang, Yan of Zhongshan, Jing of Langye, and Zheng of Donghai came to court.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 11, first month of spring: the kings Fu of Pei, Ying of Chu, Kang of Jinan, Cang of Dongping, Yan of Huaiyang, Yan of Zhongshan, Jing of Langye, and Zheng of Donghai came to audience.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，司隸校尉郭霸下獄死。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the seventh month, the metropolitan commandant Guo Ba entered prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of autumn Metropolitan Commandant Guo Ba was jailed and died there.

sentences.zh: 是歲，漅湖出黃金，廬江太守以獻。

translations.literal: That year, gold appeared from Chao Lake, and the governor of Lujiang presented it.
translations.idiomatic: That year gold surfaced in Chao Lake; the governor of Lujiang sent it to court.

sentences.zh: 時，麒麟、白雉、醴泉、嘉禾所在出焉。

translations.literal: At that time qilin, white pheasants, sweet springs, and auspicious grain appeared in various places.
translations.idiomatic: Qilin, white pheasants, sweet springs, and exceptional grain were reported from several regions.

sentences.zh: 十二年春正月，益州徼外夷哀牢王相率內屬，於是置永昌郡，罷益州西部都尉。

translations.literal: In the twelfth year, in spring, in the first month, the barbarians beyond the passes of Yizhou, the Ailao king and his followers, submitted in succession and came under Han rule; thereupon Yongchang commandery was established and the western colonel of Yizhou was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 12, first month of spring: the Ailao tribes beyond the Yizhou frontier pledged allegiance en masse; the court created Yongchang commandery and abolished the western colonel of Yizhou.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，遣將作謁者王吳修汴渠，自滎陽至於千乘海口。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fourth month, the director of works yeoman Wang Wu was sent to repair the Bian canal, from Xingyang to the estuary at Qiansheng.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of summer Wang Wu of the works directorate was ordered to restore the Bian canal from Xingyang to the sea mouth near Qiansheng.

sentences.zh: 五月丙辰，賜天下男子爵，人二級，三老、孝悌、力田人三級，流民無名數欲占者人一級；

translations.literal: On the day bingchen in the fifth month, let there be granted to the men of the empire noble ranks, two grades per person; to the village elders, the exemplars of filial piety and fraternal duty, and the champions of diligent farming, three grades per person; to vagrant people without registered names who wish to register, one grade per person;
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen in the fifth month every man received two noble steps, three for elders and moral exemplars, one for vagrants who registered;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃、貧無家屬不能自存者粟，人三斛。

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, the solitary, the seriously ill, and the poor without family who cannot support themselves, grain, three hu per person.
translations.idiomatic: three hu of grain for each widower, widow, orphan, solitary person, the gravely disabled, and the destitute with no kin.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『昔曾、閔奉親，竭歡致養；

translations.literal: An edict stated: "In olden times Zeng and Min served their parents, exhausting joy and achieving nurture;
translations.idiomatic: An edict read: "Zengzi and Min Ziqian once served their parents with full devotion;

sentences.zh: 仲尼葬子，有棺無椁。

translations.literal: when Zhongni buried his son, there was an inner coffin but no outer coffin.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius buried his son with only an inner coffin and no outer shell.

sentences.zh: 喪貴致哀，禮存寧儉。

translations.literal: In mourning what is prized is achieving grief; in ritual what is preserved is rather economy.
translations.idiomatic: Mourning values true sorrow; rites favor restraint over display.

sentences.zh: 今百姓送終之制，競為奢靡。

translations.literal: At present among the common people the regulations for sending off the dead compete in extravagance and waste.
translations.idiomatic: Today commoners vie to bankrupt themselves on funerals.

sentences.zh: 生者無擔石之儲，而財力盡於墳土。

translations.literal: The living lack a peck or stone of stored provisions, yet wealth and strength are exhausted on tomb earth.
translations.idiomatic: Families who cannot fill a peck-measure at home pour every coin into burial mounds.

sentences.zh: 伏臘無糟糠，而牲牢兼於一奠。

translations.literal: At the winter and summer offerings they lack even chaff and bran, yet victims and grain are doubled in a single offering.
translations.idiomatic: They cannot afford meat for the seasonal festivals yet heap sacrificial beasts on one tomb service.

sentences.zh: 糜破積世之業，以供終朝之費，子孫饑寒，絕命於此，豈祖考之意哉！

translations.literal: They ruin the accumulations of generations to supply the expense of a single morning; sons and grandsons face hunger and cold and lose their lives on this account—how can this be the intent of ancestors and fathers?"
translations.idiomatic: They squander ancestral savings on a morning's display while children starve—surely the dead never wished for this."

sentences.zh: 又車服制度，恣極耳目。

translations.literal: Moreover, as for carriage and clothing regulations, they indulge to the utmost what ear and eye desire.
translations.idiomatic: Carriages and dress likewise run to every extravagance eye and ear can crave.

sentences.zh: 田荒不耕，遊食者眾。

translations.literal: Fields lie waste and are not plowed; those who wander and eat are many.
translations.idiomatic: Fields go untilled while idlers multiply.

sentences.zh: 有司其申明科禁，宜於今者，宣下郡國。』

translations.literal: Let the responsible officials clarify and announce the statutes and prohibitions, and what is suitable for the present, promulgate downward to the commanderies and principalities."
translations.idiomatic: Let officials publish the sumptuary laws that fit the times and send them to every commandery."

sentences.zh: 秋七月乙亥，司空伏恭罷。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the seventh month, on the day yihai, the minister of works Fu Gong was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On yihai in the seventh month of autumn Minister of Works Fu Gong was removed.

sentences.zh: 乙未，大司農牟融為司空。

translations.literal: On the day yiwei, the grand minister of agriculture Mou Rong became minister of works.
translations.idiomatic: On yiwei Grand Minister of Agriculture Mou Rong was appointed minister of works.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，司隸校尉王康下獄死。

translations.literal: In winter, in the tenth month, the metropolitan commandant Wang Kang entered prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter Metropolitan Commandant Wang Kang died in prison.

sentences.zh: 是歲，天下安平，人無徭役，歲比登稔，百姓殷富，粟斛三十，牛羊被野。

translations.literal: That year, the empire was peaceful and secure; the people had no corvée labor; year by year the harvest ripened; the hundred surnames were rich and full; grain was thirty cash per hu; cattle and sheep covered the wilds.
translations.idiomatic: That year the realm was at peace, corvée was light, harvests ran in succession, grain sold for thirty cash a hu, and cattle and sheep blanketed the pastures.

sentences.zh: 十三年春二月，帝耕於藉田。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year, in spring, in the second month, the emperor plowed at the sacred field.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 13, second month of spring: the emperor turned the sacred furrow.

sentences.zh: 禮畢，賜觀者食。

translations.literal: When the ceremony was finished, he granted food to the spectators.
translations.idiomatic: After the rite he fed the crowd that had watched.

sentences.zh: 三月，河南尹薛昭下獄死。

translations.literal: In the third month, Xue Zhao, governor of Henan, entered prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Xue Zhao, governor of Henan, was imprisoned and died.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，汴渠成。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fourth month, the Bian canal was completed.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of summer the Bian canal works were finished.

sentences.zh: 辛巳，行幸滎陽，巡行河渠。

translations.literal: On the day xinsi, he traveled in favor to Xingyang and inspected the river and canal works.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi he traveled to Xingyang to review the river dikes and canal.

sentences.zh: 乙酉，詔曰：『自汴渠決敗，六十餘歲，加頃年以來，雨水不時，汴流東侵，日月益甚，水門故處，皆在河中，漭瀁廣溢，莫測圻岸，蕩蕩極望，不知綱紀。

translations.literal: On the day yiyou, an edict stated: "Since the Bian canal burst and failed, more than sixty years have passed; moreover in recent years rains have not kept to their seasons; the Bian flow has shifted eastward, daily growing more severe; the old sites of the water gates are all now within the river channel; vast floods spread wide with no way to measure the banks; boundless in the distance, one knows no guideline.
translations.idiomatic: On yiyou an edict said: "More than sixty years have passed since the Bian canal broke; lately unseasonable rains have pushed its course eastward until the old sluices lie midstream in a sheet of flood with no visible bank—utter chaos.

sentences.zh: 今兗、豫之人，多被水患，乃雲縣官不先人急，好興它役。

translations.literal: Now the people of Yan and Yu for the most part suffer water disaster, and they say the court does not put men's urgent needs first but likes to raise other labor projects.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Yan and Yu are drowning in it and complain that the state ignores their plight while starting other works.

sentences.zh: 又或以為河流入汴，幽、冀蒙利，故曰左堤強則右堤傷，左右俱強則下方傷，宜任水勢所之，使人隨高而處，公家息壅塞之費，百姓無陷溺之患。

translations.literal: Moreover, some hold that when the Yellow River flows into the Bian, You and Ji profit by it; hence they say when the left embankment is strong the right embankment is injured, when both left and right are strong then what is below is injured; it is fitting to follow wherever the water's force wishes to go, causing people to dwell according to height, so that the public house ends the expense of blockage and damming and the hundred surnames have no danger of drowning.
translations.idiomatic: Some argue that diverting the river into the Bian helps You and Ji, that strengthening one levee weakens another, and that both banks should not be forced lest lowlands suffer; they say we should let the flood spread and move people to high ground, sparing the treasury endless dredging while commoners escape the deluge.

sentences.zh: 議者不同，南北異論，朕不知所從，久而不決。

translations.literal: The deliberators have not agreed; south and north differ in their discussions; I have not known which to follow, and long have I failed to decide.
translations.idiomatic: Counselors disagree—north against south—and I have hesitated too long.

sentences.zh: 今既築堤理渠，絕水立門，河、汴分流，復其舊迹，陶丘之北，漸就壤墳，故薦嘉玉絜牲，以禮河神。

translations.literal: Now that dikes have been built and channels ordered, water has been cut off and gates set up; the River and the Bian have been divided and restored to their old traces; north of Taoqiu the land gradually approaches good loam; therefore I present fine jade and clean victims to sacrifice to the river god.
translations.idiomatic: Now embankments and sluices divide the Yellow River from the Bian and restore the old courses; north of Taoqiu the silt is turning to good soil; I therefore offer jade and clean beasts to the spirit of the river.

sentences.zh: 東過洛汭，嘆禹之績。

translations.literal: Passing east beyond the Luo ford, I sigh at Yu's achievement.
translations.idiomatic: East of the Luo ford I pause to honor Yu's labors.

sentences.zh: 今五土之宜，反其正色，濱渠下田，賦與貧人，無令豪右得固其利，庶繼世宗《瓠子》之作。

translations.literal: Now the suitability of the five soils has returned to its correct colors; the fields along the canal below—grant them to the poor; do not allow powerful families to monopolize the profit; perhaps we may continue Shizong's work at 《Huzi》."
translations.idiomatic: Let the five soils regain their proper hues; assign the reclaimed bottomlands beside the canal to the needy, not to great houses, in the spirit of Emperor Wu's Huzi project."

sentences.zh: 』因遂度河。

translations.literal: " Thereupon he crossed the River.
translations.idiomatic: He then crossed the Yellow River.

sentences.zh: 登太行，進幸上黨。

translations.literal: He ascended Mount Taihang and advanced in favor to Shangdang.
translations.idiomatic: He climbed the Taihang range and continued into Shangdang.

sentences.zh: 壬寅，車駕還宮。
