translations.literal: On the day renyin, the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On renyin the court returned to the palace.

sentences.zh: 冬十月壬辰晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: In winter, in the tenth month, on the last day of the month renchen, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: At month's end on renchen in the tenth month of winter the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 三公免冠自劾。

translations.literal: The three dukes removed their caps and impeached themselves.
translations.idiomatic: The three dukes laid aside their caps and offered resignations.

sentences.zh: 制曰：『冠履勿劾。

translations.literal: The rescript stated: "As for caps and shoes, do not impeach."
translations.idiomatic: The emperor replied: "Keep your caps; no resignations."

sentences.zh: 災異屢見，咎在朕躬，憂懼遑遑，未知其方。

translations.literal: Calamities and prodigies have repeatedly appeared; the fault lies in my person; anxious and fearful, I do not know the way.
translations.idiomatic: Portents pile up; the fault is mine; I am anxious and do not know the remedy.

sentences.zh: 將有司陳事，多所隱諱，使君上壅蔽，下有不暢乎？

translations.literal: Is it that the responsible officials, when presenting affairs, for the most part conceal and hide things, causing the ruler above to be blocked and covered, and those below to lack free passage?"
translations.idiomatic: Are my ministers hiding the truth so that I am blinded and the people cannot be heard?"

sentences.zh: 昔衛有忠臣，靈公得守其位。

translations.literal: In olden times Wei had loyal ministers, and Duke Ling was able to preserve his position.
translations.idiomatic: Wei once had honest ministers, and Duke Ling kept his throne because of them.

sentences.zh: 今何以和穆陰陽，消伏災譴？

translations.literal: Now by what means shall we harmonize yin and yang and dispel and subdue disasters and reproofs?"
translations.idiomatic: How shall we harmonize yin and yang and lift this curse?"

sentences.zh: 刺史、太守詳刑理冤，存恤鰥孤，勉思職焉。』

translations.literal: Let the regional inspectors and grand administrators examine punishments and set right wrongs, preserve and pity widowers and orphans, and strive to ponder their duties."
translations.idiomatic: Let every inspector and governor review prisons, right injustice, care for the widowed and orphaned, and weigh his charge carefully."

sentences.zh: 十四年春三月甲戌，司徒虞延免，自殺。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year, in spring, in the third month, on the day jiaxu, the minister of education Yu Yan was dismissed and committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 14, jiaxu in the third month of spring: Minister of Education Yu Yan was dismissed and took his own life.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丁巳，鉅鹿太守南陽邢穆為司徒。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fourth month, on the day dingsi, Xing Mu of Nanyang, grand administrator of Julu, became minister of education.
translations.idiomatic: On dingsi in the fourth month Xing Mu of Nanyang, governor of Julu, became minister of education.

sentences.zh: 前楚王英自殺。

translations.literal: The former king of Chu, Ying, committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Ying, the former king of Chu, committed suicide.

sentences.zh: 夏五月，封故廣陵王荊子元壽為廣陵侯。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fifth month, the former king of Guangling Jing's son Yuan Shou was enfeoffed as marquis of Guangling.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month of summer Yuan Shou, son of the late Prince Jing of Guangling, was enfeoffed as Marquis of Guangling.

sentences.zh: 初作壽陵。

translations.literal: For the first time work was begun on the longevity mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: Construction began on the emperor's own tomb, Shouling.

sentences.zh: 十五年春二月庚子，東巡狩。

translations.literal: In the fifteenth year, in spring, in the second month, on the day gengzi, he toured east on a hunt.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 15, gengzi in the second month of spring: he set out on an eastern progress.

sentences.zh: 辛丑，幸偃師，詔亡命自殊死以下贖：死罪縑四十匹，右趾至髡鉗城旦舂十匹，完城旦至司寇五匹；

translations.literal: On the day xinchou, he favored Yanshi; it was ordered that fugitives from the law, down from those liable for execution by dismemberment, might ransom themselves: for death, forty bolts of fine silk; for right-toe amputation down through shaved-head wall-dawn labor, ten bolts; for intact wall-dawn labor down to minister-of-crimes labor, five bolts;
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou he stopped at Yanshi and offered ransom scales for fugitives down to capital crimes: forty bolts of silk for death, ten for mutilation sentences, five for lesser hard labor;

sentences.zh: 犯罪未發覺，詔書到日自告者，半入贖。

translations.literal: for crimes not yet discovered, those who confess themselves on the day the edict arrives pay half the ransom.
translations.idiomatic: those who surrendered when the edict arrived paid half.

sentences.zh: 徵沛王輔會睢陽。

translations.literal: The king of Pei, Fu, was summoned to meet at Suiyang.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Fu of Pei was ordered to Suiyang.

sentences.zh: 進幸彭城。

translations.literal: He advanced in favor to Pengcheng.
translations.idiomatic: He continued to Pengcheng.

sentences.zh: 癸亥，帝耕於下邳。

translations.literal: On the day guihai, the emperor plowed at Xiapi.
translations.idiomatic: On guihai he plowed the sacred field at Xiapi.

sentences.zh: 三月，徵琅邪王京會良成，徵東平王蒼會陽都，又徵廣陵侯及其三弟會魯。

translations.literal: In the third month, the king of Langye, Jing, was summoned to meet at Liangcheng; the king of Dongping, Cang, was summoned to meet at Yangdu; moreover the marquis of Guangling and his three younger brothers were summoned to meet at Lu.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Princes Jing of Langye and Cang of Dongping were summoned to Liangcheng and Yangdu, and the Marquis of Guangling with his three brothers to Lu.

sentences.zh: 祠東海恭王陵。

translations.literal: He sacrificed at the tomb of the respectful king of Donghai.
translations.idiomatic: He sacrificed at the tomb of King Gong of Donghai.

sentences.zh: 還，幸孔子宅，祠仲尼及七十二弟子。

translations.literal: Returning, he favored the house of Confucius and sacrificed to Zhongni and the seventy-two disciples.
translations.idiomatic: On the return journey he visited Confucius's home, offering sacrifice to the Master and his seventy-two disciples.

sentences.zh: 親御講堂，命皇太子、諸王說經。

translations.literal: He personally ascended the lecture hall and ordered the heir apparent and the various kings to expound the classics.
translations.idiomatic: He entered the lecture hall and had the crown prince and the kings discourse on the classics.

sentences.zh: 又幸東平。

translations.literal: He again favored Dongping.
translations.idiomatic: He visited Dongping again.

sentences.zh: 辛卯，進幸大梁，至定陶，祠定陶恭王陵。

translations.literal: On the day xinmao, he advanced in favor to Daliang, arrived at Dingtao, and sacrificed at the tomb of the respectful king of Dingtao.
translations.idiomatic: On xinmao he reached Daliang and Dingtao and worshipped at the tomb of King Gong of Dingtao.

sentences.zh: 夏四月庚子，車駕還宮。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fourth month, on the day gengzi, the imperial carriage returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the fourth month of summer the court returned to the capital.

sentences.zh: 改信都為樂成國，臨淮為下邳國。

translations.literal: Xindu was changed to be the state of Lecheng, and Linhuai was changed to be the state of Xiapi.
translations.idiomatic: Xindu commandery became the kingdom of Lecheng and Linhuai became the kingdom of Xiapi.

sentences.zh: 封皇子恭為鉅鹿王、黨為樂成王、衍為下邳王、暢為汝南王、丙為常山王、長為濟陰王。

translations.literal: The imperial sons Gong were enfeoffed as king of Julu, Dang as king of Lecheng, Yan as king of Xiapi, Chang as king of Runan, Bing as king of Changshan, and Chang as king of Jiyin.
translations.idiomatic: Princes Gong, Dang, Yan, Chang, Bing, and Chang received Julu, Lecheng, Xiapi, Runan, Changshan, and Jiyin.

sentences.zh: 賜天下男子爵，人三級；

translations.literal: Let there be granted to the men of the empire noble ranks, three grades per person;
translations.idiomatic: Every man in the realm received three steps of noble rank;

sentences.zh: 郎、從官視事二十歲已上帛百匹，十歲已上二十匹，十歲已下十匹，官府吏五匹，書佐、小史三匹。

translations.literal: to gentlemen-of-the-palace and attendants in office twenty years or more, one hundred bolts of silk each; ten years or more, twenty bolts; below ten years, ten bolts; to government office clerks, five bolts; to secretary clerks and minor scribes, three bolts.
translations.idiomatic: palace gentlemen and attendants of twenty years' service received a hundred bolts of silk, ten-year men twenty, under ten ten bolts; bureau clerks five; scribes three.

sentences.zh: 令天下大酺五日。

translations.literal: It was ordered that the empire hold a general drinking feast for five days.
translations.idiomatic: The court ordered five days of public feasting across the realm.

sentences.zh: 乙巳，大赦天下，其謀反大逆及諸不應宥者，皆赦除之。

translations.literal: On the day yisi, there was a great amnesty of the empire; as for those guilty of rebellion and great treason and the various cases not fit for forgiveness, all were pardoned and remitted.
translations.idiomatic: On yisi came a sweeping amnesty that even covered many capital crimes previously excluded.

sentences.zh: 冬，車騎校獵上林苑。

translations.literal: In winter, the chariots and horse guards conducted a battue hunt in the Shanglin Park.
translations.idiomatic: In winter the imperial hunt was held in Shanglin Park.

sentences.zh: 十二月，遣奉車都尉竇固、駙馬都尉耿秉屯涼州。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, the bearer of the imperial coach Dou Gu and the consort-escort commandant Geng Bing were sent to encamp at Liangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Dou Gu, bearer of the coach, and Geng Bing, consort-escort commandant, were posted to Liangzhou.

sentences.zh: 十六年春二月，遣太僕祭肜出高闕，奉車都尉竇固出酒泉，駙馬都尉耿秉出居延，騎都尉來苗出平城，伐北匈奴。

translations.literal: In the sixteenth year, in spring, in the second month, the grand coachman Ji Yong was sent out through Gaoyu, the bearer of the imperial coach Dou Gu out through Jiuquan, the consort-escort commandant Geng Bing out through Juyan, the chief commandant of cavalry Lai Miao out through Pingcheng, to attack the Northern Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 16, second month of spring: Ji Yong marched from Gaoyu, Dou Gu from Jiuquan, Geng Bing from Juyan, and Lai Miao from Pingcheng against the Northern Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 竇固破呼衍王於天山，留兵屯伊吾廬城。

translations.literal: Dou Gu defeated the King Huyan at Tianshan and left troops encamped at the city of Yiwu Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Gu broke the Huyan king at Tianshan and garrisoned Yiwu.

sentences.zh: 耿秉、來苗、祭肜並無功而還。

translations.literal: Geng Bing, Lai Miao, and Ji Yong together had no achievement and returned.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Bing, Lai Miao, and Ji Yong achieved nothing and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 夏五月，淮陽王延謀反，發覺。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fifth month, the king of Huaiyang, Yan, plotted rebellion; it was discovered.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month of summer Prince Yan of Huaiyang plotted revolt; the plot was uncovered.

sentences.zh: 癸丑，司徒邢穆、駙馬都尉韓光坐事下獄死，所連及誅死者甚眾。

translations.literal: On the day guichou, the minister of education Xing Mu and the consort-escort commandant Han Guang, sitting for their affairs, entered prison and died; those connected and implicated, executed, were very many.
translations.idiomatic: On guichou Minister Xing Mu and Han Guang, consort-escort commandant, were jailed for their part in the affair and died; a host of others were executed for complicity.

sentences.zh: 戊午晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On the last day of the month wuwu, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: At month's end on wuwu the sun was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 六月丙寅，大司農西河王敏為司徒。

translations.literal: On the day bingyin in the sixth month, Wang Min of Xihe, grand minister of agriculture, became minister of education.
translations.idiomatic: On bingyin in the sixth month Wang Min of Xihe, grand minister of agriculture, became minister of education.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，淮陽王延徙封阜陵王。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the seventh month, the king of Huaiyang, Yan, was moved in enfeoffment to be king of Fuling.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of autumn Prince Yan of Huaiyang was demoted to king of Fuling.

sentences.zh: 九月丁卯，詔令郡國中都官死罪繫囚減死罪一等，勿笞，詣軍營，屯朔方、敦煌；

translations.literal: On the day dingmao in the ninth month, it was ordered that capital prisoners in the commanderies and principalities under sentence of death have their death sentence reduced one grade, not be flogged, and go to military camps, encamped at Shuofang and Dunhuang;
translations.idiomatic: On dingmao in the ninth month death-row convicts in the provinces were commuted one degree, spared the rod, and sent to garrison Shuofang and Dunhuang;

sentences.zh: 妻子自隨，父母同產欲求從者，恣聽之；

translations.literal: wives and children might follow of their own accord; where parents or full siblings wished to seek to follow, let them be heard as they will;
translations.idiomatic: wives and children might follow; parents or full brothers who wished to join them were free to do so;

sentences.zh: 女子嫁為人妻，勿與俱。

translations.literal: women married as someone's wife were not to go with them.
translations.idiomatic: married daughters were not to accompany them.

sentences.zh: 謀反大逆無道不用此書。

translations.literal: For rebellion and great treason against the Way, this writ is not used.
translations.idiomatic: The order did not apply to treason and lese-majesty.

sentences.zh: 是歲，北匈奴寇雲中，雲中太守廉范擊破之。

translations.literal: That year, the Northern Xiongnu raided Yunzhong; Lian Fan, grand administrator of Yunzhong, attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: That year the Northern Xiongnu struck Yunzhong; Governor Lian Fan repulsed them.

sentences.zh: 十七年春正月，甘露降於甘陵。

translations.literal: In the seventeenth year, in spring, in the first month, sweet dew fell on Ganling.
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 17, first month of spring: sweet dew was reported at Ganling.

sentences.zh: 北海王睦薨。

translations.literal: The king of Beihai, Mu, died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Mu, king of Beihai, died.

sentences.zh: 二月乙巳，司徒王敏薨。

translations.literal: On the day yisi in the second month, the minister of education Wang Min died.
translations.idiomatic: On yisi in the second month Minister of Education Wang Min died.

sentences.zh: 三月癸丑，汝南太守鮑昱為司徒。

translations.literal: On the day guichou in the third month, Bao Yu, grand administrator of Runan, became minister of education.
translations.idiomatic: On guichou in the third month Bao Yu, governor of Runan, was appointed minister of education.

sentences.zh: 是歲，甘露仍降，樹枝內附，芝草生殿前，神雀五色翔集京師。

translations.literal: That year, sweet dew repeatedly fell; tree branches bent inward in submission; lingzhi grew before the hall; divine sparrows of five colors flew and gathered in the capital.
translations.idiomatic: That year sweet dew fell again and again, boughs bent inward in token of submission, lingzhi sprouted before the palace, and five-colored "divine sparrows" flocked to the capital.

sentences.zh: 西南夷哀牢、儋耳、僬僥、槃木、白狼、動黏諸種，前後慕義貢獻；

translations.literal: The Yi of the southwest—Ailao, Dan'er, Jiaojiao, Panmu, Bailang, Dongnian, and the various stocks—in succession admired righteousness and presented tribute;
translations.idiomatic: Southwestern peoples—the Ailao, Dan'er, Jiaojiao, Panmu, Bailang, Dongnian, and other tribes—sent tribute one after another in token of devotion;

sentences.zh: 西域諸國遣子入侍。

translations.literal: the various states of the Western Regions sent sons to attend at court.
translations.idiomatic: and the Western Regions sent royal hostages to the Han court.

sentences.zh: 夏五月戊子，公卿百官以帝威德懷遠，祥物顯應，乃並集朝堂，奉觴上壽。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fifth month, on the day wuzi, because the emperor's awesome virtue cherished the distant and auspicious things had manifestly responded, the dukes, ministers, and hundred officials together gathered in the court hall, raised goblets, and offered longevity wishes.
translations.idiomatic: On wuzi in the fifth month of summer the high ministers, citing the emperor's power to win the far barbarians and Heaven's clear answer in omens, thronged the hall, raised their cups, and wished him long life.

sentences.zh: 制曰：『天生神物，以應王者；

translations.literal: The rescript stated: "Heaven produces numinous things in order to answer to the king;
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "Heaven sends prodigies to signal a true king;

sentences.zh: 遠人慕化，實由有德。

translations.literal: distant people admiring transformation truly proceeds from possessing virtue.
translations.idiomatic: distant peoples come to civilization only when the throne is virtuous.

sentences.zh: 朕以虛薄，何以享斯？

translations.literal: I, being hollow and slight, by what means do I enjoy this?"
translations.idiomatic: I am shallow and unworthy—how dare I claim such honors?"

sentences.zh: 唯高祖、光武聖德所被，不敢有辭。

translations.literal: Only what Gaozu and Guangwu, by their sage virtue, extended—I dare not decline it.
translations.idiomatic: I accept only because the sage virtue of Gaozu and Guangwu draws these signs; I cannot refuse that legacy.

sentences.zh: 其敬舉觴，太常擇吉日策告宗廟。

translations.literal: Respectfully raise the goblets; let the chamberlain for ceremonials choose an auspicious day, document, and report to the ancestral temples.
translations.idiomatic: Let the cups be raised in due form, and let the minister of rituals choose a lucky day to memorialize the ancestors in the imperial shrines.

sentences.zh: 其賜天下男子爵，人二級，三老、孝悌、力田人三級，流人無名數欲占者人一級；

translations.literal: Let there be granted to the men of the empire noble ranks, two grades per person; to the village elders, the exemplars of filial piety and fraternal duty, and the champions of diligent farming, three grades per person; to vagrant people without registered names who wish to register, one grade per person;
translations.idiomatic: Grant two noble steps to every man, three to elders and moral exemplars, one to vagrants who take up registered residence;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃、貧不能自存者粟，人三斛；

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, the solitary, the seriously ill, and the poor unable to support themselves, grain, three hu per person;
translations.idiomatic: three hu of grain to each widower, widow, orphan, solitary soul, the gravely disabled, and the destitute;

sentences.zh: 郎、從官視事十歲以上者，帛十匹。

translations.literal: to gentlemen-of-the-palace and attendants who have been in office ten years or more, ten bolts of silk each;
translations.idiomatic: ten bolts of silk to every palace gentleman or attendant with ten years of service;

sentences.zh: 中二千石、二千石下至黃綬，貶秩奉贖，在去年以來皆還贖。』

translations.literal: from those of attendant two thousand piculs and two thousand piculs down to holders of the yellow ribbon, those who suffered demotion in rank or used salary to ransom offenses—since last year, in every case return the ransom payments."
translations.idiomatic: refund all fines and salary-forfeitures paid since last year by officials from two thousand piculs down to yellow-seal rank."

sentences.zh: 秋八月丙寅，令武威、張掖、酒泉、敦煌及張掖屬國，繫囚右趾已下任兵者，皆一切勿治其罪，詣軍營。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the eighth month, on the day bingyin, it was ordered that in Wuwei, Zhangye, Jiuquan, Dunhuang, and the Zhangye dependent state, for all prisoners bound for military service from right-toe amputation downward, in every case do not prosecute their crimes further; send them to military camps.
translations.idiomatic: On bingyin in the eighth month of autumn convicts in Wuwei, Zhangye, Jiuquan, Dunhuang, and the Zhangye dependent state who had been sentenced to military labor short of death were pardoned of further prosecution and marched to the camps.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月，遣奉車都尉竇固、駙馬都尉耿秉、騎都尉劉張出敦煌昆侖塞，擊破白山虜於蒲類海上，遂入車師。

translations.literal: In winter, in the eleventh month, the bearer of the imperial coach Dou Gu, the consort-escort commandant Geng Bing, and the chief commandant of cavalry Liu Zhang were sent out through the Kunlun Pass of Dunhuang, attacked and defeated the White Mountain barbarians on the Pulei Sea, and thereupon entered Cheshi.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month of winter Dou Gu, Geng Bing, and Liu Zhang marched from Dunhuang through Kunlun Pass, destroyed the White Mountain tribes on Lake Pulei, and pushed into Cheshi.

sentences.zh: 初置西域都護、戊己校尉。

translations.literal: For the first time the protector-general of the Western Regions and the colonels of Wu and Ji were established.
translations.idiomatic: The court first appointed a protector-general of the Western Regions and Wu-Ji colonels to hold the frontier.

sentences.zh: 是歲，改天水為漢陽郡。

translations.literal: That year, Tianshui was changed to be Hanyang commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Tianshui commandery was renamed Hanyang.

sentences.zh: 十八年春三月丁亥，詔曰：『其令天下亡命，自殊死已下贖：死罪縑三十匹，右趾至髡鉗城旦舂十匹，完城旦至司寇五匹；

translations.literal: In the eighteenth year, in spring, in the third month, on the day dinghai, an edict stated: "Let it be ordered that fugitives from the law in the empire, from those liable for execution by dismemberment downward, may ransom themselves: for death, thirty bolts of fine silk; for right-toe amputation down through shaved-head wall-dawn labor, ten bolts; for intact wall-dawn labor down to minister-of-crimes labor, five bolts;
translations.idiomatic: Yongping 18, dinghai in the third month of spring: fugitives facing death or lesser capital crimes might buy release for thirty bolts of silk down to five for minor hard labor;

sentences.zh: 吏人犯罪未發覺，詔書到自告者，半入贖。』

translations.literal: for clerks and people whose crimes have not yet been discovered, those who confess themselves when the edict arrives pay half the ransom."
translations.idiomatic: officials and commoners who surrendered when the edict arrived paid half the listed sums."

sentences.zh: 夏四月己未，詔曰：『自春已來，時雨不降，宿麥傷旱，秋種未下，政失厥中，憂懼而已。

translations.literal: In summer, in the fourth month, on the day jiwei, an edict stated: "From spring onward timely rains have not fallen; the winter wheat has been injured by drought; the autumn planting has not gone in; government has missed the mean; I am anxious and fearful, that is all.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei in the fourth month of summer an edict said: "Since spring the seasonal rains have failed; winter wheat is withering; autumn seed cannot go into the ground; policy has lost the middle way, and I am anxious.

sentences.zh: 其賜天下男子爵，人二級，及流民無名數欲占者人一級；

translations.literal: Let there be granted to the men of the empire noble ranks, two grades per person, and to vagrant people without registered names who wish to register, one grade per person;
translations.idiomatic: Let every man receive two steps of rank and every unregistered wanderer who settles one step;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃、貧不能自存者粟，人三斛。

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, the solitary, the seriously ill, and the poor unable to support themselves, grain, three hu per person.
translations.idiomatic: grant three hu of grain to each widower, widow, orphan, solitary person, the gravely disabled, and the destitute.

sentences.zh: 理冤獄，錄輕繫。

translations.literal: Set right wrongful imprisonment; record and release those lightly bound.
translations.idiomatic: Review unjust jailings and free those held on light charges.

sentences.zh: 二千石分禱五嶽四瀆。

translations.literal: Let those of two thousand piculs divide prayers among the five marchmounts and four great rivers.
translations.idiomatic: Let each two-thousand-picul official pray at his region's great mountains and rivers.

sentences.zh: 郡界有名山大川能興雲致雨者，長吏各絜齋禱請，冀蒙嘉澍。』

translations.literal: Where within a commandery's borders there are famous mountains and great rivers able to raise clouds and bring down rain, let the senior officials each purify themselves by fasting and pray and request, hoping to receive fine soaking rains."
translations.idiomatic: Where a county holds peaks and streams famed for rain, let the magistrate fast and pray for a blessed downpour."

sentences.zh: 六月己未，有星孛於太微。

translations.literal: On the day jiwei in the sixth month, there was a broom star in Supreme Tenuity.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei in the sixth month a comet appeared in the Supreme Tenuity asterism.

sentences.zh: 焉耆、龜茲攻西域都護陳睦，悉沒其眾。

translations.literal: Yanqi and Qiuci attacked the protector-general of the Western Regions, Chen Mu, and utterly destroyed his forces.
translations.idiomatic: Yanqi and Kucha attacked Protector-General Chen Mu and annihilated his army.

sentences.zh: 北匈奴及車師後王圍戊己校尉耿恭。

translations.literal: The Northern Xiongnu and the rear king of Cheshi besieged the Wu-Ji colonel Geng Gong.
translations.idiomatic: The Northern Xiongnu and the rear king of Cheshi surrounded Colonel Geng Gong.

sentences.zh: 秋八月壬子，帝崩於東宮前殿。

translations.literal: In autumn, in the eighth month, on the day renzi, the emperor died in the front hall of the Eastern Palace.
translations.idiomatic: On renzi in the eighth month of autumn the emperor died in the front hall of the Eastern Palace.

sentences.zh: 年四十八。

translations.literal: He was forty-eight sui.
translations.idiomatic: He was forty-eight years old.

sentences.zh: 遺詔無起寢廟，藏主於光烈皇后更衣別室。

translations.literal: His testamentary edict ordered that no resting temple be raised; the spirit tablet was deposited in the separate chamber for changing dress of Empress Guanglie.
translations.idiomatic: His final edict forbade a separate imperial temple; his spirit tablet was housed with Empress Guanglie's in her robing side-chamber.

sentences.zh: 帝初作壽陵，制令流水而已，石椁廣一丈二尺，長二丈五尺，無得起墳。

translations.literal: When the emperor first built his longevity mausoleum, the regulations ordered only flowing water; the stone outer coffin was to be one zhang two chi in breadth and two zhang five chi in length; there was to be no raising of a burial mound.
translations.idiomatic: When he first planned Shouling he decreed a plain grave with running water only, a stone outer coffin twelve feet wide and twenty-five long, and no piled tumulus.

sentences.zh: 萬年之後，埽地而祭，杅水脯糒而已。

translations.literal: After the myriad years, sweep the ground and sacrifice; only well water, dried meat, and dried rice are to be used.
translations.idiomatic: After his death offerings should be only a swept floor, well water, dried meat, and meal cakes.

sentences.zh: 過百日，唯四時設奠，置吏卒數人供給灑埽，勿開修道。

translations.literal: After passing a hundred days, only at the four seasons set out offerings; place several clerk-soldiers to supply sprinkling and sweeping; do not open or repair roads.
translations.idiomatic: After the hundred days' mourning only the seasonal libations should continue, with a few men to keep the site swept; do not build new approach roads.

sentences.zh: 敢有所興作者，以擅議宗廟法從事。

translations.literal: Whoever dares to undertake any construction shall be dealt with according to the law against presumptuously discussing the ancestral temples.
translations.idiomatic: Anyone who raises new works on the tomb shall be prosecuted under the statute forbidding unauthorized changes to imperial shrines.

sentences.zh: 帝遵奉建武制度，無敢違者。

translations.literal: The emperor reverently upheld the institutions of Jianwu; none dared transgress them.
translations.idiomatic: He held strictly to the Jianwu precedents, and no one dared overstep them.

sentences.zh: 後宮之家，不得封侯與政。

translations.literal: As for families of the rear palace, they were not to be enfeoffed as marquises or allowed to participate in government.
translations.idiomatic: Consort kin were barred from marquisates and from meddling in policy.

sentences.zh: 館陶公主為子求郎，不許，而賜錢千萬。

translations.literal: The princess of Guantao begged a gentleman-of-the-palace post for her son; he did not grant it but bestowed ten million cash.
translations.idiomatic: The Princess of Guantao asked a palace cadetship for her son; he refused but gave her ten million cash instead.

sentences.zh: 謂群臣曰：『郎官上應列宿，出宰百里，茍非其人，則民受其殃，是以難之。

translations.literal: He said to the host of ministers: "Gentlemen of the Palace correspond to the asterisms above; when sent out they govern a territory of a hundred li; if they are not the right men, then the people will receive disaster—therefore I find it difficult."
translations.idiomatic: He told his ministers: "A gentleman of the Palace matches a star in Heaven; sent out he rules a hundred li. The wrong appointment ruins the people—that is why I hesitate to hand out such posts."

sentences.zh: 』故吏稱其官，民安其業，遠近肅服，戶口滋殖焉。

translations.literal: Therefore clerks praised their offices, the people were secure in their employments; far and near were solemnly submissive, and household registration grew and multiplied.
translations.idiomatic: Hence officials took pride in their titles, commoners kept to their trades, the realm stood in awe, and the census rolls swelled.

sentences.zh: 論曰：明帝善刑理，法令分明。

translations.literal: The assessment states: Emperor Ming was good at punishments and principles; statutes and orders were clear and distinct.
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: Emperor Ming understood law and government; his regulations were lucid.

sentences.zh: 日晏坐朝，幽枉必達。

translations.literal: When the sun was late he still sat at court; hidden wrongs were sure to reach him.
translations.idiomatic: He held court into the gloaming so that even hidden grievances reached the throne.

sentences.zh: 內外無倖曲之私，在上無矜大之色。

translations.literal: Within and without there was no favoritism of bending and twisting; above there was no color of self-important greatness.
translations.idiomatic: Court and countryside knew no crooked favoritism, and he wore no haughty air.

sentences.zh: 斷獄得情，號居前代十二。

translations.literal: In deciding cases he obtained the true situation; his reputation ranked twelve parts above that of former generations.
translations.idiomatic: His judgments caught the truth; later ages ranked him far above earlier emperors in that art.

sentences.zh: 故後之言事者，莫不先建武、永平之政。

translations.literal: Therefore among those who later spoke on affairs, none failed to cite first the government of Jianwu and Yongping.
translations.idiomatic: Later reformers always invoked Jianwu and Yongping as their model.

sentences.zh: 而鐘離意、宋均之徒，常以察慧為言，夫豈弘人之度未優乎？

translations.literal: Yet men such as Zhongli Yi and Song Jun constantly spoke in terms of scrutiny and cleverness—was it perhaps that the measure of magnanimity toward others was not yet ample?"
translations.idiomatic: Yet critics like Zhongli Yi and Song Jun harped on petty scrutiny—perhaps his large-mindedness still fell short of the ideal?"

sentences.zh: 贊曰：顯宗丕承，業業兢兢。

translations.literal: The encomium states: Xianzong grandly received the legacy, fearful and trembling.
translations.idiomatic: The verse praise runs: "Xianzong inherited the great charge in trembling awe.

sentences.zh: 危心恭德，政察姦勝。

translations.literal: With wary mind and reverent virtue, his government was scrutinizing and overcame treachery.
translations.idiomatic: Mindful and reverent, his rule was strict and broke treason.

sentences.zh: 備章朝物，省薄墳陵。

translations.literal: He completed the regalia of court and curtailed the thickness of tomb mounds.
translations.idiomatic: He perfected court ritual and kept his tomb plain.

sentences.zh: 永懷廢典，下身遵道。

translations.literal: Forever cherishing neglected canons, he lowered his person to follow the Way.
translations.idiomatic: He revived forgotten rites and humbled himself before the Way.

sentences.zh: 登臺觀雲，臨雍拜老。

translations.literal: He ascended the terrace to observe the clouds; he attended the Hong Academy and bowed to the aged.
translations.idiomatic: He climbed the Spirit Terrace to read the clouds and entered the Hong gate to honor the old.

sentences.zh: 懋惟帝績，增光文考。

translations.literal: Earnestly he pondered the imperial achievement and added luster to his cultured father.
translations.idiomatic: He built on the imperial legacy and brought fresh glory to his father Wen."
